[["currentConversationId","pins","agentExecutionMode","isPanelCollapsed","displayedAnnouncements","conversations","onboarding","a|0|1|2|3|4|5|6","b7605a04-1e0c-4f94-8dd9-a79d0df90140","o|","auto","b|T","a|","a|8","id","createdAtIso","lastInteractedAtIso","selectedModelId","requestIds","isPinned","isShareable","extraData","personaType","feedbackStates","rootTaskUuid","name","toolUseStates","chatHistory","draftExchange","a|E|F|G|H|I|J|K|L|M|N|O|P|Q|R|S","2026-02-20T12:48:54.547Z","2026-04-11T16:59:05.005Z","claude-opus-4-6","b|F","isAgentConversation","baselineTimestamp","a|Y|Z","n|VGV5wyM","o|a|B|b","n|0","acf72c20-8427-473f-b596-5515721e9600","PaperSense Backend Bug Fixes & Enhancements","request_id","uuid","chatItemType","status","fromTimestamp","toTimestamp","seen_state","a|g|h|i|j|k|l|m","c8357d2a-5a9e-43d8-a501-7c102b9be6ee","8c1ad856-5f03-4bcb-9a37-4daee6bdfc72","agentic-checkpoint-delimiter","success","n|VBlqN3N","n|VBlqexu","seen","o|n|o|p|q|r|s|t|u","exchangeUuid","timestamp","request_message","a|i|w|x|y|j|m","exchange-pointer","76e310a0-38d4-4256-b108-d6f4af2a93a0","2026-02-20T12:50:02.595Z","Role : professional AI Engineer
Main Idea :

Researchers face information overload as the number of academic papers grows rapidly. Traditional keyword search engines often return long lists of results without context, making it difficult to extract key findings, methodologies, and limitations. This thesis proposes building a Smart Research Paper Analyzer that combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Large Language Models (LLMs) to deliver context-aware summaries, structured insights, and citation-linked answers directly from research papers.

How It Will Be Implemented:
The pipeline will include PDF parsing, chunking, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and LLM-based generation. 
•	Open-source stack: SentenceTransformers, Qdrant/Chroma, Llama 3/Mistral/Gemini.

•	Frontend : Next.js + React, pdf.js , TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui.
•	API : FastAPI

","o|z|10|11|12|13|r|u","revertTarget","a|g|h|i|j|k|l|15|m","cd88c094-ed49-449d-9539-5de7774918f7","535497bf-2371-40cb-9859-e4e469d3c999","n|VBlqfil","a|h","o|1A|p","o|16|17|18|q|r|t|19|1B|u","a|g|j|i","f6f9f5e3-2b42-410f-a6d5-bb4c31c2e7d5","cancelled","agentic-turn-delimiter","o|1D|1E|1F|1G","5d9d0508-4f29-4ee0-95d4-771e70d727d0","2026-02-20T12:50:07.881Z","","o|z|10|1I|1J|1K|1F|u","396db762-60db-4e14-b4f9-1bb6156c8336","2026-02-20T12:51:23.991Z","Main Idea :
Researchers face information overload as the number of academic papers grows rapidly. Traditional keyword search engines often return long lists of results without context, making it difficult to extract key findings, methodologies, and limitations. This thesis proposes building a Smart Research Paper Analyzer that combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Large Language Models (LLMs) to deliver context-aware summaries, structured insights, and citation-linked answers directly from research papers.
Motivation:
Such a system would significantly reduce the time needed for literature review, support evidence-based research, and improve accessibility of academic knowledge. By providing citation-grounded summaries, it addresses the common issue of hallucination in LLMs, ensuring reliability.
Where It Is Applied:
The tool will be applied in academic research, higher education, and R&D-driven industries where rapid synthesis of scientific literature is essential.
How It Will Be Implemented:
The pipeline will include PDF parsing, chunking, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and LLM-based generation. 
•	Open-source stack: SentenceTransformers, Qdrant/Chroma, Llama 3/Mistral.
•	Frontend : Next.js + React, pdf.js , TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui.
•	API : FastAPI

 1	"""
 2	Test script for the Smart Research Paper Analyzer API

 4	import requests

 7	
 8	BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"

 11	def test_health():
 12	 """Test health endpoint"""

 14	 response = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/health")
 15	 print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
 16	 print(f"Response: {json.dumps(response.json(), indent=2)}")

 20	def test_upload(pdf_path: str):
 21	 """Test document upload"""
 22	 print("\n=== Testing Document Upload ===")
 23	 
 24	 if not Path(pdf_path).exists():

 26	 return None
 27	 
 28	 with open(pdf_path, 'rb') as f:
 29	 files = {'file': (Path(pdf_path).name, f, 'application/pdf')}
 30	 response = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/upload", files=files)
 31

 33	 result = response.json()
 34	 print(f"Response: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}")
 35	 
 36	 if result.get('success'):
 37	 return result.get('document_id')

 50	 if document_id:

 58	 print(f"Citations: {len(result.get('citations', []))}")
 59	 return response.status_code == 200

 61	
 62	def test_analyze(document_id: str):
 63	 """Test document analysis"""
 64	 print("\n=== Testing Document Analysis ===")
 65	 
 66	 response = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/analyze/{document_id}")
 67	 print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
 68	 result = response.json()
 69	 
 70	 if result.get('success'):
 71	 print(f"Summary: {result.get('summary', 'N/A')[:200]}...")
 72	 print(f"Key Findings: {len(result.get('key_findings', []))} found")
 73	 print(f"Methodology: {result.get('methodology', 'N/A')[:200]}...")
 74	 print(f"Limitations: {len(result.get('limitations', []))} found")
 75	 else:
 76	 print(f"Error: {result.get('error')}")
 77	 
 78	 return response.status_code == 200

 80

 84	 
 85	 response = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/stats")
 86	 print(f"Status: {response.status_code}")
 87	 result = response.json()
 88	 print(f"Response: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}")
 89	 return response.status_code == 200

 91

 1	"""
 2	FastAPI main application for Smart Research Paper Analyzer
 3	"""
 4	from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, File, HTTPException
 5	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
 6	from loguru import logger
 7	import sys
 8	
 9	from config import settings
 10	from services.rag_pipeline import RAGPipeline
 11	from models.schemas import (
 12	 DocumentUploadResponse, QueryRequest, QueryResponse,
 13	 AnalysisResponse, DeleteResponse, StatsResponse, HealthResponse
 14	)
 15	
 16	# Configure logging
 17	logger.remove()
 18	logger.add(sys.stderr, level="INFO" if not settings.debug else "DEBUG")
 19	logger.add("logs/app.log", rotation="500 MB", level="DEBUG")
 20	
 21	# Initialize FastAPI app
 22	app = FastAPI(
 23	 title=settings.app_name,
 24	 version=settings.app_version,
 25	 description="RAG-based research paper analyzer with Qdrant, OpenAI, and Gemini"
 26	)
 27	
 28	# Configure CORS
 29	app.add_middleware(
 30	 CORSMiddleware,
 31	 allow_origins=["*"], # Configure appropriately for production
 32	 allow_credentials=True,
 33	 allow_methods=["*"],
 34	 allow_headers=["*"],
 35	)
 36	
 37	# Initialize RAG pipeline
 38	rag_pipeline = RAGPipeline()
 39	
 40	logger.info(f"Starting {settings.app_name} v{settings.app_version}")
 41	
 42	
 43	@app.get("/", response_model=HealthResponse)

 46	 return {
 47	 "status": "healthy",

 51	 "embeddings": "openai",
 52	 "llm": "gemini"
 53	 }
 54	 }

 56

 60	 return {
 61	 "status": "healthy",
 62	 "version": settings.app_version,

 65	 "embeddings": "openai",
 66	 "llm": "gemini"
 67	 }
 68	 }

 70	
 71	@app.post("/upload", response_model=DocumentUploadResponse)
 72	async def upload_document(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
 73	 """
 74	 Upload and process a research paper PDF
 75	 
 76	 Args:
 77	 file: PDF file to upload
 78	 
 79	 Returns:
 80	 Processing result with document_id
 81	 """
 82	 try:
 83	 # Validate file type
 84	 if not file.filename.endswith('.pdf'):
 85	 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Only PDF files are supported")
 86	 
 87	 # Read file content
 88	 content = await file.read()
 89	 
 90	 if len(content) == 0:
 91	 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Empty file")
 92	 
 93	 logger.info(f"Received file: {file.filename}, size: {len(content)} bytes")
 94	 
 95	 # Process document through RAG pipeline
 96	 result = rag_pipeline.process_document(content, file.filename)
 97	 
 98	 if not result.get("success"):
 99	 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=result.get("error", "Processing failed"))
 100	 
 101	 return result
 102	 
 103	 except HTTPException:
 104	 raise
 105	 except Exception as e:
 106	 logger.error(f"Upload error: {e}")
 107	 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
 108	
 109

 119	 Answer with citations

 128	 return result
 129

 132	 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))
 133	
 134

Let me check the RAG pipeline's `analyze_document` method.","","o|7J|99|9A","o|7F|d|7G|1K|9B|7Q","toolu_vrtx_01V8sCqG7jhuxfHG7RGC41X1","{"path": "backend/services/rag_pipeline.py"}","o|7a|9D|7c|9E|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|9F|9","n|2P","n|3dy","n|aK","o|6f|6D|9H|9I|9J","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|9K","a|9C|9G|9L|8x|8y|8z","2026-03-19T17:33:20.925Z","o|8b|1K|93|94|98|9M|W|9N|r|X|u","bb18e08e-f1ad-45fc-9a3b-464000cb9850","Here's the result of running `cat -n` on backend/services/rag_pipeline.py:
 1	"""
 2	RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Pipeline
 3	Combines all services for end-to-end document processing and querying
 4	"""
 5	from typing import Dict, List, Optional
 6	import uuid
 7	from loguru import logger
 8	
 9	from services.pdf_parser import PDFParser
 10	from services.chunker import Chunker
 11	from services.embedding_service import EmbeddingService
 12	from services.vector_store import VectorStore
 13	from services.llm_service import LLMService
 14	from config import settings
 15	
 16	
 17	class RAGPipeline:
 18	 """Main RAG pipeline orchestrating all services"""
 19	 
 20	 def __init__(self):
 21	 self.pdf_parser = PDFParser()
 22	 self.text_chunker = Chunker(
 23	 chunk_size=settings.chunk_size,
 24	 chunk_overlap=settings.chunk_overlap
 25	 )
 26	 self.embedding_service = EmbeddingService()
 27	 self.vector_store = VectorStore()
 28	 self.llm_service = LLMService()
 29	 
 30	 logger.info("RAG Pipeline initialized")
 31	 
 32	 def process_document(self, pdf_file: bytes, filename: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 33	 """
 34	 Process a PDF document through the entire pipeline
 35	 
 36	 Args:
 37	 pdf_file: PDF file as bytes
 38	 filename: Original filename
 39	 
 40	 Returns:
 41	 Processing result with document_id and statistics
 42	 """
 43	 try:
 44	 # Generate unique document ID
 45	 document_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
 46	 logger.info(f"Processing document {filename} with ID {document_id}")
 47	 
 48	 # Step 1: Parse PDF
 49	 logger.info("Step 1: Parsing PDF")
 50	 parsed_data = self.pdf_parser.parse_pdf(pdf_file, filename)
 51	 
 52	 if not parsed_data['text']:
 53	 return {
 54	 "success": False,
 55	 "error": "Failed to extract text from PDF"
 56	 }
 57	 
 58	 # Step 2: Chunk text
 59	 logger.info("Step 2: Chunking text")
 60	 doc_metadata = {
 61	 "filename": filename,
 62	 "title": parsed_data['metadata'].get('title', 'Unknown'),
 63	 "author": parsed_data['metadata'].get('author', 'Unknown'),
 64	 "page_count": parsed_data['page_count']
 65	 }
 66	
 67	 chunks = self.text_chunker.chunk_text(
 68	 parsed_data['text'],
 69	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 70	 )
 71	
 72	 # Step 2b: Create table chunks
 73	 tables = parsed_data.get('tables', [])
 74	 table_chunks = self.text_chunker.create_table_chunks(
 75	 tables,
 76	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 77	 start_chunk_id=len(chunks),
 78	 )
 79	 chunks.extend(table_chunks)
 80	
 81	 # Step 2c: Create image chunks
 82	 images = parsed_data.get('images', [])
 83	 image_chunks = self.text_chunker.create_image_chunks(
 84	 images,
 85	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 86	 start_chunk_id=len(chunks),
 87	 )
 88	 chunks.extend(image_chunks)
 89	
 90	 if not chunks:
 91	 return {
 92	 "success": False,
 93	 "error": "Failed to create chunks"
 94	 }
 95	
 96	 # Step 3: Generate embeddings
 97	 logger.info("Step 3: Generating embeddings")
 98	 chunks_with_embeddings = self.embedding_service.embed_chunks(chunks)
 99	
 100	 # Step 4: Store in vector database
 101	 logger.info("Step 4: Storing in vector database")
 102	 success = self.vector_store.add_documents(chunks_with_embeddings, document_id)
 103	
 104	 if not success:
 105	 return {
 106	 "success": False,
 107	 "error": "Failed to store in vector database"
 108	 }
 109	
 110	 # Return success with statistics
 111	 return {
 112	 "success": True,
 113	 "document_id": document_id,
 114	 "filename": filename,
 115	 "metadata": parsed_data['metadata'],
 116	 "statistics": {
 117	 "page_count": parsed_data['page_count'],
 118	 "char_count": parsed_data['char_count'],
 119	 "chunk_count": len(chunks),
 120	 "table_count": len(table_chunks),
 121	 "image_count": len(image_chunks),
 122	 }
 123	 }
 124	 
 125	 except Exception as e:
 126	 logger.error(f"Document processing error: {e}")
 127	 return {
 128	 "success": False,
 129	 "error": str(e)
 130	 }
 131	 
 132	 def query(
 133	 self, 
 134	 question: str, 
 135	 document_id: Optional[str] = None,
 136	 top_k: int = None
 137	 ) -> Dict[str, any]:
 138	 """
 139	 Query the knowledge base with a question
 140

 156	 logger.info("Step 1: Generating query embedding")
 157	 query_embedding = self.embedding_service.generate_embedding(question)

 192	 def analyze_document(self, document_id: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 193	 """
 194	 Perform comprehensive analysis of a document
 195	
 196	 Args:
 197	 document_id: Document to analyze
 198	
 199	 Returns:
 200	 Analysis results including summary, findings, methodology, limitations
 201	 """
 202	 try:
 203	 logger.info(f"Analyzing document {document_id}")
 204	
 205	 # Retrieve all chunks for the document
 206	 # Use a dummy query vector to get all chunks (search with high top_k)
 207	 dummy_embedding = [0.0] * 1536
 208	 all_chunks = self.vector_store.search(
 209	 query_vector=dummy_embedding,
 210	 top_k=1000, # Get many chunks
 211	 document_id=document_id,
 212	 score_threshold=0.0 # No threshold
 213	 )
 214	
 215	 if not all_chunks:
 216	 return {
 217	 "success": False,
 218	 "error": "Document not found"
 219	 }
 220	
 221	 # Separate chunks by content type
 222	 text_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type', 'text') == 'text']
 223	 table_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type') == 'table']
 224	 image_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type') == 'image']
 225	
 226	 # Combine text from chunks (use first 20 text chunks)
 227	 full_text = " ".join([chunk['text'] for chunk in text_chunks[:20]])
 228	
 229	 # Append table data to context so the LLM can reference it
 230	 if table_chunks:
 231	 table_text = "\n\n".join([c['text'] for c in table_chunks[:10]])
 232	 full_text += f"\n\n--- TABLES FOUND IN THE PAPER ---\n{table_text}"
 233	
 234	 # Append image/figure captions
 235	 if image_chunks:
 236	 fig_text = "\n".join([c['text'] for c in image_chunks[:10]])
 237	 full_text += f"\n\n--- FIGURES/IMAGES IN THE PAPER ---\n{fig_text}"
 238	
 239	 # Generate various analyses
 240	 logger.info("Generating summary")
 241	 summary = self.llm_service.generate_summary(full_text)
 242	
 243	 logger.info("Extracting key findings")
 244	 key_findings = self.llm_service.extract_key_findings(full_text)
 245	
 246	 logger.info("Identifying methodology")
 247	 methodology = self.llm_service.identify_methodology(full_text)
 248	
 249	 logger.info("Extracting limitations")
 250	 limitations = self.llm_service.extract_limitations(full_text)
 251	
 252	 return {
 253	 "success": True,
 254	 "document_id": document_id,
 255	 "summary": summary,
 256	 "key_findings": key_findings,
 257	 "methodology": methodology,
 258	 "limitations": limitations,
 259	 "metadata": all_chunks[0].get('metadata', {}) if all_chunks else {}
 260	 }
 261	
 262	 except Exception as e:
 263	 logger.error(f"Analysis error: {e}")
 264	 return {
 265	 "success": False,
 266	 "error": str(e)
 267	 }

The 404 is being raised at line 150 of `main.py` when `result.get("success")` is False. This means `analyze_document` is being called, but the vector store search returns no chunks for that document_id.

The issue is in the `analyze_document` method in `rag_pipeline.py` at line 207-213. It uses a dummy zero vector `[0.0] * 1536` with `score_threshold=0.0`. The problem with the zero vector is that cosine similarity with a zero vector is undefined (division by zero), which could result in no results being returned. But also, the `score_threshold=0.0` might filter out results.
 
So the 404 isn't from the route not being defined—it's the endpoint itself returning a 404 because the vector search fails to find any matching chunks for that document.

The problem is likely that using a zero vector with `score_threshold=0.0` causes Qdrant to either return no results (since cosine similarity with a zero vector is undefined) or filter everything out, making it impossible to retrieve any chunks for the analysis.

 1	"""
 2	PDF parsing service for extracting text from research papers.
 3	
 4	Uses Docling as the primary parser for advanced extraction of structured text,
 5	tables, and images. Falls back to pdfplumber / PyPDF2 when Docling is
 6	unavailable or fails.
 7	"""
 8	import base64
 9	import io
 10	import tempfile
 11	import os
 12	from typing import Dict, List, Optional
 13	
 14	import PyPDF2
 15	import pdfplumber
 16	from loguru import logger
 17	
 18	# Docling imports – wrapped so the rest of the module still works if docling
 19	# is not installed (graceful degradation).
 20	try:
 21	 from docling.document_converter import DocumentConverter, PdfFormatOption
 22	 from docling.datamodel.base_models import InputFormat, DocumentStream
 23	 from docling.datamodel.pipeline_options import PdfPipelineOptions
 24	 from docling_core.types.doc import PictureItem, TableItem
 25	
 26	 DOCLING_AVAILABLE = True
 27	except ImportError:
 28	 DOCLING_AVAILABLE = False
 29	 logger.warning(
 30	 "Docling is not installed – advanced PDF parsing (tables, images) "
 31	 "will be unavailable. Install with: pip install docling"
 32	 )
 33	
 34	
 35	class PDFParser:
 36	 """Extract and structure text from PDF research papers"""
 37	
 38	 def __init__(self):
 39	 self.supported_formats = ['.pdf']
 40	
 41	 # Lazily initialised Docling converter (heavy model download on first use)
 42	 self._docling_converter: Optional["DocumentConverter"] = None
 43	
 44	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 45	 # Docling helpers
 46	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 47	
 48	 def _get_docling_converter(self) -> "DocumentConverter":
 49	 """Return a cached DocumentConverter, creating it on first call."""
 50	 if self._docling_converter is None:
 51	 pipeline_options = PdfPipelineOptions()
 52	 pipeline_options.do_table_structure = True
 53	 pipeline_options.generate_picture_images = True
 54	 pipeline_options.generate_page_images = False # not needed for RAG
 55	 pipeline_options.images_scale = 2.0
 56	
 57	 self._docling_converter = DocumentConverter(
 58	 format_options={
 59	 InputFormat.PDF: PdfFormatOption(
 60	 pipeline_options=pipeline_options
 61	 )
 62	 }
 63	 )
 64	 return self._docling_converter
 65	
 66	 def extract_with_docling(self, pdf_file: bytes, filename: str = "document.pdf") -> Dict[str, any]:
 67	 """
 68	 Parse a PDF with Docling and return structured results including
 69	 markdown text, extracted tables (as markdown), and base64-encoded
 70	 images of figures found in the document.
 71	
 72	 Returns a dict with keys:
 73	 - text: full document exported as Markdown
 74	 - tables: list of dicts with table markdown and optional dataframe CSV
 75	 - images: list of dicts with base64 PNG data and captions
 76	 """
 77	 converter = self._get_docling_converter()
 78	
 79	 # Docling can accept a DocumentStream for in-memory bytes
 80	 buf = io.BytesIO(pdf_file)
 81	 source = DocumentStream(name=filename, stream=buf)
 82	
 83	 conv_result = converter.convert(source)
 84	 doc = conv_result.document
 85	
 86	 # --- Full text as Markdown ---
 87	 markdown_text = doc.export_to_markdown()
 88	
 89	 # --- Tables ---
 90	 tables: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
 91	 for idx, table in enumerate(doc.tables):
 92	 table_data: Dict[str, str] = {
 93	 "index": idx,
 94	 "html": table.export_to_html(doc=doc),
 95	 }
 96	 try:
 97	 import pandas as pd
 98	 df = table.export_to_dataframe(doc=doc)
 99	 table_data["csv"] = df.to_csv(index=False)
 100	 table_data["markdown"] = df.to_markdown(index=False)
 101	 except Exception:
 102	 table_data["csv"] = ""
 103	 table_data["markdown"] = ""
 104	 tables.append(table_data)
 105	
 106	 # --- Images / Figures ---
 107	 images: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
 108	 for element, _level in doc.iterate_items():
 109	 if isinstance(element, PictureItem):
 110	 try:
 111	 pil_image = element.get_image(doc)
 112	 if pil_image is not None:
 113	 img_buffer = io.BytesIO()
 114	 pil_image.save(img_buffer, format="PNG")
 115	 img_b64 = base64.b64encode(img_buffer.getvalue()).decode("utf-8")
 116	 images.append({
 117	 "base64_png": img_b64,
 118	 "caption": getattr(element, "caption", "") or "",
 119	 })
 120	 except Exception as img_err:
 121	 logger.warning(f"Could not extract image: {img_err}")
 122	
 123	 return {
 124	 "text": markdown_text,
 125	 "tables": tables,
 126	 "images": images,
 127	 }
 128	
 129	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 130	 # Legacy parsers (kept as fallbacks)
 131	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 132	
 133	 def extract_text_pypdf2(self, pdf_file: bytes) -> str:
 134	 """Extract text using PyPDF2"""
 135	 try:
 136	 pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_file))
 137	 text = ""
 138	 for page in pdf_reader.pages:
 139	 text += page.extract_text() + "\n"
 140	 return text
 141	 except Exception as e:
 142	 logger.error(f"PyPDF2 extraction failed: {e}")
 143	 return ""
 144	
 145	 def extract_text_pdfplumber(self, pdf_file: bytes) -> str:
 146	 """Extract text using pdfplumber (better for complex layouts)"""
 147	 try:
 148	 text = ""
 149	 with pdfplumber.open(io.BytesIO(pdf_file)) as pdf:
 150	 for page in pdf.pages:
 151	 page_text = page.extract_text()
 152	 if page_text:
 153	 text += page_text + "\n"
 154	 return text
 155	 except Exception as e:
 156	 logger.error(f"pdfplumber extraction failed: {e}")
 157	 return ""
 158	
 159	 def extract_metadata(self, pdf_file: bytes) -> Dict[str, str]:
 160	 """Extract metadata from PDF"""
 161	 try:
 162	 pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_file))
 163	 metadata = pdf_reader.metadata
 164	 return {
 165	 "title": metadata.get("/Title", "Unknown"),
 166	 "author": metadata.get("/Author", "Unknown"),
 167	 "subject": metadata.get("/Subject", ""),
 168	 "creator": metadata.get("/Creator", ""),
 169	 "producer": metadata.get("/Producer", ""),
 170	 "creation_date": str(metadata.get("/CreationDate", "")),
 171	 }
 172	 except Exception as e:
 173	 logger.error(f"Metadata extraction failed: {e}")
 174	 return {}
 175	
 176	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 177	 # Main entry point
 178	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 179	
 180	 def parse_pdf(self, pdf_file: bytes, filename: str = "") -> Dict[str, any]:
 181	 """
 182	 Main parsing method that extracts text, tables, images, and metadata.
 183	
 184	 Strategy:
 185	 1. Try Docling first (best quality – structured text, tables, images).
 186	 2. Fall back to pdfplumber → PyPDF2 for plain text if Docling fails.
 187	
 188	 Args:
 189	 pdf_file: PDF file as bytes
 190	 filename: Original filename
 191	
 192	 Returns:
 193	 Dictionary with text, metadata, page_count, tables, images, etc.
 194	 """
 195	 logger.info(f"Parsing PDF: {filename}")
 196	
 197	 tables: List[Dict] = []
 198	 images: List[Dict] = []
 199	 text = ""
 200	
 201	 # --- Attempt Docling extraction ---
 202	 if DOCLING_AVAILABLE:
 203	 try:
 204	 logger.info("Using Docling for advanced PDF parsing")
 205	 docling_result = self.extract_with_docling(pdf_file, filename or "document.pdf")
 206	 text = docling_result.get("text", "")
 207	 tables = docling_result.get("tables", [])
 208	 images = docling_result.get("images", [])
 209	 logger.info(
 210	 f"Docling extracted {len(text)} chars, "
 211	 f"{len(tables)} tables, {len(images)} images"
 212	 )
 213	 except Exception as e:
 214	 logger.error(f"Docling extraction failed, falling back: {e}")
 215	 text = ""
 216	
 217	 # --- Fallback to legacy parsers if Docling produced no text ---
 218	 if not text or len(text.strip()) < 100:
 219	 logger.warning("Docling text insufficient, trying pdfplumber")
 220	 text = self.extract_text_pdfplumber(pdf_file)
 221	
 222	 if not text or len(text.strip()) < 100:
 223	 logger.warning("pdfplumber extraction poor, trying PyPDF2")
 224	 text = self.extract_text_pypdf2(pdf_file)
 225	
 226	 metadata = self.extract_metadata(pdf_file)
 227	
 228	 # Count pages
 229	 try:
 230	 pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_file))
 231	 page_count = len(pdf_reader.pages)
 232	 except Exception:
 233	 page_count = 0
 234	
 235	 return {
 236	 "text": text,
 237	 "metadata": metadata,
 238	 "page_count": page_count,
 239	 "filename": filename,
 240	 "char_count": len(text),
 241	 "tables": tables,
 242	 "images": images,
 243	 }
 244

","o|8g|Cb|X|BM|BU","o|8e|6D|6D|Cc","a|Cd|6W","The `std::bad_alloc` errors are memory allocation failures happening in Docling's preprocessing stage for certain pages (19-30). This is likely because:

1. `images_scale = 2.0` doubles the resolution of images, consuming much more memory
2. `generate_picture_images = True` forces image extraction which uses a lot of memory
3. Processing all pages at once causes memory spikes

The fix should:
1. Reduce `images_scale` from 2.0 to something lower (1.0)
2. Catch the `std::bad_alloc` errors gracefully - but these are C++ errors that might crash the process


3. Disable `generate_picture_images` since it's the most memory-intensive operation, or process pages in batches to avoid memory spikes on complex pages like those in the 19-30 range where the preprocessing stage is failing.

The real solution is to disable OCR entirely since this appears to be a standard PDF that doesn't need character recognition, which would eliminate the memory overhead from loading those Chinese language models. Combined with reducing the image scale and disabling picture generation, this should bring memory usage down enough to handle the full document. I'm also wondering if Docling's pipeline has options to limit the number of pages processed or set memory constraints upfront, which could prevent the worker from crashing entirely.","","o|7J|Cf|Cg","o|7F|d|7G|1K|Ch|7Q","toolu_vrtx_01NnHfdPjZqV1DgDod2ZAifD","web-search","{"query": "docling PdfPipelineOptions reduce memory usage std::bad_alloc do_ocr images_scale 2024 2025"}","o|7a|Cj|Ck|Cl|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|Cm|9","toolu_vrtx_01Pvad1YNbaRD6K5cyPK5jDq","codebase-retrieval","{"information_request": "How is the Docling DocumentConverter configured? What PdfPipelineOptions are available? Check config.py for any related settings."}","o|7a|Co|Cp|Cq|1K|1K","o|7T|6K|7U|1K|Cr|9","n|IU","n|r1","o|6f|6D|Ct|BZ|Cu","o|6Z|6K|6a|1K|Cv","o|6l|6L|d|1K|9","a|Ci|Cn|Cs|Cw|Cx|7p|7r","2026-03-19T17:42:44.212Z","o|8b|1K|93|Ca|Ce|Cy|W|Cz|r|X|u","
The `std::bad_alloc` is a C++ out-of-memory crash in Docling's native preprocessing pipeline. The current config is very memory-hungry:
- `images_scale = 2.0` quadruples pixel count sent to the layout model
- `generate_picture_images = True` forces full figure image generation
- OCR is enabled by default (loads 3 RapidOCR models into memory)

When enough pages fail, the worker process crashes and kills the client connection.

 131	 # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 132	
 133	 def extract_text_pypdf2(self, pdf_file: bytes) -> str:
 134	 """Extract text using PyPDF2"""
 135	 try:
 136	 pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_file))
 137	 text = ""
 138	 for page in pdf_reader.pages:
 139	 text += page.extract_text() + "\n"
 140	 return text
 141	 except Exception as e:
 142	 logger.error(f"PyPDF2 extraction failed: {e}")
 143	 return ""
 144	
 145	 def extract_text_pdfplumber(self, pdf_file: bytes) -> str:
 146	 """Extract text using pdfplumber (better for complex layouts)"""
 147	 try:
 148	 text = ""
 149	 with pdfplumber.open(io.BytesIO(pdf_file)) as pdf:
 150	 for page in pdf.pages:
 151	 page_text = page.extract_text()
 152	 if page_text:
 153	 text += page_text + "\n"
 154	 return text
 155	 except Exception as e:
 156	 logger.error(f"pdfplumber extraction failed: {e}")
 157	 return ""
...
 179	
 180	 def parse_pdf(self, pdf_file: bytes, filename: str = "") -> Dict[str, any]:
 181	 """
 182	 Main parsing method that extracts text, tables, images, and metadata.
 183	
 184	 Strategy:
 185	 1. Try Docling first (best quality – structured text, tables, images).
 186	 2. Fall back to pdfplumber → PyPDF2 for plain text if Docling fails.
 187	
 188	 Args:
 189	 pdf_file: PDF file as bytes
 190	 filename: Original filename
 191	
 192	 Returns:
 193	 Dictionary with text, metadata, page_count, tables, images, etc.
 194	 """
 195	 logger.info(f"Parsing PDF: {filename}")
 196	
 197	 tables: List[Dict] = []
 198	 images: List[Dict] = []
 199	 text = ""
 200	
 201	 # --- Attempt Docling extraction ---
 202	 if DOCLING_AVAILABLE:
 203	 try:
 204	 logger.info("Using Docling for advanced PDF parsing")
 205	 docling_result = self.extract_with_docling(pdf_file, filename or "document.pdf")
 206	 text = docling_result.get("text", "")
 207	 tables = docling_result.get("tables", [])
 208	 images = docling_result.get("images", [])
 209	 logger.info(
 210	 f"Docling extracted {len(text)} chars, "
 211	 f"{len(tables)} tables, {len(images)} images"
 212	 )
 213	 except Exception as e:
 214	 logger.error(f"Docling extraction failed, falling back: {e}")
 215	 text = ""
 216	
 217	 # --- Fallback to legacy parsers if Docling produced no text ---
 218	 if not text or len(text.strip()) < 100:
 219	 logger.warning("Docling text insufficient, trying pdfplumber")
 220	 text = self.extract_text_pdfplumber(pdf_file)
 221	
 222	 if not text or len(text.strip()) < 100:
 223	 logger.warning("pdfplumber extraction poor, trying PyPDF2")
 224	 text = self.extract_text_pypdf2(pdf_file)
 225	
 226	 metadata = self.extract_metadata(pdf_file)
 227	
 228	 # Count pages
 229	 try:
 230	 pdf_reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(io.BytesIO(pdf_file))
 231	 page_count = len(pdf_reader.pages)
 232	 except Exception:
 233	 page_count = 0
 234	
 235	 return {
 236	 "text": text,
 237	 "metadata": metadata,
 238	 "page_count": page_count,
 239	 "filename": filename,
 240	 "char_count": len(text),
 241	 "tables": tables,
 242	 "images": images,
 243	 }
 244

Path: backend/README.md

 38	
 39	## Tech Stack
 40	
 41	- **Framework**: FastAPI
 42	- **Vector Database**: Qdrant
 43	- **Embeddings**: OpenAI (text-embedding-3-small)
 44	- **LLM**: Google Gemini Pro
 45	- **PDF Processing**: PyPDF2, pdfplumber
 46	- **Configuration**: Pydantic Settings
 47	
 48	## Setup
 49	
 50	### Prerequisites
 51	
 52	- Python 3.9+
 53	- Qdrant (running locally or cloud)
 54	- OpenAI API key
 55	- Google Gemini API key
 56	
 57	### Installation
 58	
 59	1. Install dependencies:
 60	```bash
 61	pip install -r requirements.txt
 62	```
 63	
 64	2. Configure environment variables:
 65	```bash
 66	cp .env .env
 67	# Edit .env with your API keys
 68	```
 69	
 70	3. Start Qdrant (if running locally):
 71	```bash
 72	docker run -p 6333:6333 qdrant/qdrant
 73	```
 74	
 75	4. Run the application:
 76	```bash
 77	python main.py
 78	```
 79	
 80	Or with uvicorn:
 81	```bash
 82	uvicorn main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
 83	```
...
 118	
 119	### Analyze Document
 120	```bash
 121	curl -X GET "http://localhost:8000/analyze/{document_id}"
 122	```
 123	
 124	## Configuration
 125	
 126	Edit `.env` file:
 127	
 128	```env
 129	# API Keys
 130	GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_gemini_api_key
 131	OPENAI_API_KEY=your_openai_api_key
 132	
 133	# Qdrant
 134	QDRANT_HOST=localhost
 135	QDRANT_PORT=6333
 136	QDRANT_COLLECTION_NAME=research_papers
 137	
 138	# Models
 139	EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small
 140	GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-pro
 141	CHUNK_SIZE=1000
 142	CHUNK_OVERLAP=200
 143	```
 144	
 145	## Project Structure
...

Path: backend/config.py
 1	"""
 2	Configuration management for the Smart Research Paper Analyzer
 3	"""
 4	from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
 5	from pydantic import Field
 6	from typing import Optional
 7	
 8	
 9	class Settings(BaseSettings):
 10	 """Application settings loaded from environment variables"""
 11	 
 12	 # API Keys
 13	 google_api_key: str = Field(..., env="GOOGLE_API_KEY")
 14	 openai_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(None, env="OPENAI_API_KEY")
 15	 
 16	 # Qdrant Configuration
 17	 qdrant_host: str = Field(default="localhost", env="QDRANT_HOST")
 18	 qdrant_port: int = Field(default=6333, env="QDRANT_PORT")
 19	 qdrant_api_key: Optional[str] = Field(default=None, env="QDRANT_API_KEY")
 20	 qdrant_collection_name: str = Field(default="research_papers", env="QDRANT_COLLECTION_NAME")
 21	 
 22	 # Application Settings
 23	 app_name: str = Field(default="Smart Research Paper Analyzer", env="APP_NAME")
 24	 app_version: str = Field(default="1.0.0", env="APP_VERSION")
 25	 debug: bool = Field(default=True, env="DEBUG")
 26	 
 27	 # Model Configuration
 28	 embedding_model: str = Field(default="text-embedding-3-small", env="EMBEDDING_MODEL")
 29	 gemini_model: str = Field(default="gemini-pro", env="GEMINI_MODEL")
 30	 chunk_size: int = Field(default=1000, env="CHUNK_SIZE")
 31	 chunk_overlap: int = Field(default=200, env="CHUNK_OVERLAP")
 32	 
 33	 # Server Configuration
 34	 host: str = Field(default="0.0.0.0", env="HOST")
 35	 port: int = Field(default=8000, env="PORT")
 36	 
 37	 # Vector Search Configuration
 38	 top_k_results: int = Field(default=5, env="TOP_K_RESULTS")
 39	 similarity_threshold: float = Field(default=0.3, env="SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD")
 40	 
 41	 class Config:
 42	 env_file = ".env"
 43	 env_file_encoding = "utf-8"
 44	 case_sensitive = False
 45	
 46	
 47	# Global settings instance
 48	settings = Settings()
 49

 2	Pydantic models for API request/response schemas

 14	 statistics: Optional[Dict] = None
 15	 error: Optional[str] = None

 17

 21	 document_id: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Specific document to search")
 22	 top_k: Optional[int] = Field(5, description="Number of chunks to retrieve")

 1	"""
 2	LLM service using Google Gemini API for text generation
 3	Updated to use the latest google.genai library
 4	"""
 5	from typing import List, Dict, Optional
 6	from google import genai
 7	from google.genai import types
 8	from loguru import logger
 9	from config import settings
 10	
 11	
 12	class LLMService:
 13	 """Google Gemini LLM service for generation using google.genai"""
 14	
 15	 def __init__(self):
 16	 # Initialize the Gemini client with API key
 17	 self.client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.google_api_key)
 18	 self.model_name = settings.gemini_model
 19	
 20	 # Default generation configuration
 21	 self.default_config = types.GenerateContentConfig(
 22	 temperature=0.7,
 23	 top_p=0.95,
 24	 top_k=40,
 25	 max_output_tokens=2048,
 26	 response_modalities=["TEXT"],
 27	 )

 184	
 185	## Development Tips
 186	
 187	1. **Auto-reload**: The server auto-reloads when you change code (with `--reload` flag)
 188	2. **Logs**: Check `logs/app.log` for detailed debugging
 189	3. **API Docs**: Visit `/docs` for interactive API documentation
 190	4. **Qdrant UI**: Visit `http://localhost:6333/dashboard` to inspect vectors
 191	
 192	## Production Deployment
 193	
 194	For production:
 195	1. Set `DEBUG=False` in `.env`
 196	2. Use a production WSGI server (gunicorn)
 197	3. Set up proper CORS origins
 198	4. Use managed Qdrant Cloud
 199	5. Implement authentication
 200	6. Add rate limiting
 201	
 202	## Support
 203	
 204	For issues or questions:
 205	1. Check the logs in `logs/app.log`
 206	2. Verify all services are running
 207	3. Test with the provided `test_api.py` script
 208

","o|8g|D6|X|Ca|Co","o|8e|6K|6D|D7","a|D5|D8|8n","The `std::bad_alloc` errors are out-of-memory crashes happening in Docling's native C++ preprocessing pipeline. The current config is very memory-hungry:

1. `images_scale = 2.0` — doubles the resolution, quadrupling memory for image processing
2. `generate_picture_images = True` — generates full images of every figure
3. OCR is enabled by default (RapidOCR loads 3 models), which adds memory pressure

For a 30+ page PDF, this causes pages 19-30 to fail with `std::bad_alloc`, and potentially crashes the uvicorn worker process entirely ("api cuts connection").


The main strategy is to aggressively reduce memory usage: scale images down to 1.0, skip picture generation since we only need text and captions for RAG, and disable OCR for PDFs that already have extractable text. I should also check if PdfPipelineOptions has a `do_ocr` flag to control this directly. key changes:
1. `images_scale = 1.0` (was 2.0)
2. `generate_picture_images = False` (was True) — we don't need base64 images for RAG
3. Add `do_ocr = False` to skip OCR (saves memory, not needed for born-digital PDFs)

Since `generate_picture_images = False`, the image extraction loop in `extract_with_docling` will produce no images, which is fine. We still get table structure and markdown text.

The `std::bad_alloc` is happening during the preprocess stage when the layout model runs, not during image extraction. Looking at the logs, RapidOCR is loading Chinese models which adds significant memory overhead, and `images_scale = 2.0` means the page images sent to the layout model are at 2x resolution. Reducing `images_scale` to 1.0 and disabling OCR should have the most impact since they directly control what the layout analysis model consumes. **Problem:** Docling's preprocessing runs a layout-analysis model on rasterized page images. With `images_scale = 2.0`, each page image is 4× the pixel count, and combined with OCR (3 RapidOCR models loaded into RAM) and full figure-image generation, pages 19–30 hit `std::bad_alloc` (C++ OOM). Enough failures crash the uvicorn worker, killing the client connection.

**Changes in `backend/services/pdf_parser.py`:**

1. **`images_scale` → `1.0`** (was `2.0`) — halves the width/height of page images sent to the layout model, cutting memory ~4×.
2. **`generate_picture_images` → `False`** (was `True`) — skips rendering full-resolution figure images; captions are still extracted.
3. **`do_ocr` → `False`** — skips loading 3 OCR models (~300 MB+ RAM). Born-digital PDFs (like research papers) already have extractable text and don't need OCR.
4. **Image extraction loop updated** — gracefully handles the case where `get_image()` returns `None` (since picture generation is off), still captures figure captions for the RAG pipeline.

Restart your server and try uploading the same PDF again. The memory pressure should be dramatically lower and the pages that were failing should now parse successfully.

 1	"""
 2	Embedding service using OpenAI for vector generation
 3	"""
 4	from typing import List, Dict
 5	import openai
 6	from loguru import logger
 7	from config import settings
 8	
 9	
 10	class EmbeddingService:
 11	 """Generate embeddings using OpenAI API"""
 12	 
 13	 def __init__(self):
 14	 self.client = openai.OpenAI(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
 15	 self.model = settings.embedding_model
 16	 self.embedding_dimension = 1536 # Default for text-embedding-3-small
 17	 
 18	 def generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
 19	 """
 20	 Generate embedding for a single text
 21	 
 22	 Args:
 23	 text: Input text to embed
 24	 
 25	 Returns:
 26	 List of floats representing the embedding vector
 27	 """
 28	 try:
 29	 response = self.client.embeddings.create(
 30	 model=self.model,
 31	 input=text
 32	 )
 33	 embedding = response.data[0].embedding
 34	 logger.debug(f"Generated embedding of dimension {len(embedding)}")
 35	 return embedding
 36	 except Exception as e:
 37	 logger.error(f"Embedding generation failed: {e}")
 38	 raise
 39	 
 40	 def generate_embeddings_batch(self, texts: List[str], batch_size: int = 100) -> List[List[float]]:
 41	 """
 42	 Generate embeddings for multiple texts in batches
 43	 
 44	 Args:
 45	 texts: List of texts to embed
 46	 batch_size: Number of texts to process in each batch
 47	 
 48	 Returns:
 49	 List of embedding vectors
 50	 """
 51	 embeddings = []
 52	 
 53	 for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size):
 54	 batch = texts[i:i + batch_size]
 55	 logger.info(f"Processing batch {i // batch_size + 1}, size: {len(batch)}")
 56	 
 57	 try:
 58	 response = self.client.embeddings.create(
 59	 model=self.model,
 60	 input=batch
 61	 )
 62	 batch_embeddings = [item.embedding for item in response.data]
 63	 embeddings.extend(batch_embeddings)
 64	 except Exception as e:
 65	 logger.error(f"Batch embedding failed: {e}")
 66	 # Fallback to individual processing
 67	 for text in batch:
 68	 try:
 69	 emb = self.generate_embedding(text)
 70	 embeddings.append(emb)
 71	 except:
 72	 logger.error(f"Failed to embed text: {text[:50]}...")
 73	 embeddings.append([0.0] * self.embedding_dimension)
 74	 
 75	 logger.info(f"Generated {len(embeddings)} embeddings")
 76	 return embeddings
 77	 
 78	 def embed_chunks(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 79	 """
 80	 Add embeddings to chunk dictionaries
 81	 
 82	 Args:
 83	 chunks: List of chunk dictionaries
 84	 
 85	 Returns:
 86	 Chunks with added 'embedding' field
 87	 """
 88	 texts = [chunk['text'] for chunk in chunks]
 89	 embeddings = self.generate_embeddings_batch(texts)
 90	 
 91	 for chunk, embedding in zip(chunks, embeddings):
 92	 chunk['embedding'] = embedding
 93	 
 94	 return chunks
 95	
 96	
 97	
 98	########################## LANGCHAIN EMBEDDING ######################################
 99	#todo: implement langchain embedding under here
 100

 1	"""
 2	Text chunking service for splitting documents into semantic chunks
 3	"""
 4	from typing import List, Dict
 5	import re
 6	from loguru import logger
 7	
 8	
 9	class Chunker:
 10	 """Intelligent text chunking for research papers"""
 11	 
 12	 def __init__(self, chunk_size: int = 1000, chunk_overlap: int = 200):
 13	 self.chunk_size = chunk_size
 14	 self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap
 15	 
 16	 def clean_text(self, text: str) -> str:
 17	 """Clean and normalize text"""
 18	 # Remove excessive whitespace
 19	 text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
 20	 # Remove page numbers and headers/footers patterns
 21	 text = re.sub(r'\n\d+\n', '\n', text)
 22	 return text.strip()
 23	 
 24	 def detect_sections(self, text: str) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
 25	 """
 26	 Detect common research paper sections
 27	 Returns list of sections with their content
 28	 """
 29	 sections = []
 30	 
 31	 # Common section headers in research papers
 32	 section_patterns = [
 33	 r'\n(Abstract|ABSTRACT)\s*\n',
 34	 r'\n(Introduction|INTRODUCTION)\s*\n',
 35	 r'\n(Related Work|RELATED WORK|Literature Review)\s*\n',
 36	 r'\n(Methodology|METHODOLOGY|Methods|METHODS)\s*\n',
 37	 r'\n(Results|RESULTS)\s*\n',
 38	 r'\n(Discussion|DISCUSSION)\s*\n',
 39	 r'\n(Conclusion|CONCLUSION|Conclusions)\s*\n',
 40	 r'\n(References|REFERENCES)\s*\n',
 41	 ]
 42	 
 43	 # Find all section boundaries
 44	 boundaries = []
 45	 for pattern in section_patterns:
 46	 matches = re.finditer(pattern, text)
 47	 for match in matches:
 48	 boundaries.append({
 49	 'position': match.start(),
 50	 'title': match.group(1)
 51	 })
 52	 
 53	 # Sort by position
 54	 boundaries.sort(key=lambda x: x['position'])
 55	 
 56	 # Extract sections
 57	 for i, boundary in enumerate(boundaries):
 58	 start = boundary['position']
 59	 end = boundaries[i + 1]['position'] if i + 1 < len(boundaries) else len(text)
 60	 sections.append({
 61	 'title': boundary['title'],
 62	 'content': text[start:end].strip()
 63	 })
 64	 
 65	 return sections
 66	 
 67	 def chunk_by_sentences(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
 68	 """Split text into chunks while preserving sentence boundaries"""
 69	 # Split into sentences
 70	 sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', text)
 71	 
 72	 chunks = []
 73	 current_chunk = ""
 74	 
 75	 for sentence in sentences:
 76	 # If adding this sentence exceeds chunk_size, save current chunk
 77	 if len(current_chunk) + len(sentence) > self.chunk_size and current_chunk:
 78	 chunks.append(current_chunk.strip())
 79	 # Start new chunk with overlap
 80	 overlap_text = current_chunk[-self.chunk_overlap:] if len(current_chunk) > self.chunk_overlap else current_chunk
 81	 current_chunk = overlap_text + " " + sentence
 82	 else:
 83	 current_chunk += " " + sentence
 84	 
 85	 # Add the last chunk
 86	 if current_chunk.strip():
 87	 chunks.append(current_chunk.strip())
 88	 
 89	 return chunks
 90	 
 91	 def chunk_text(self, text: str, metadata: Dict = None) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 92	 """
 93	 Main chunking method that creates semantic chunks
 94	
 95	 Args:
 96	 text: Input text to chunk
 97	 metadata: Optional metadata to attach to each chunk
 98	
 99	 Returns:
 100	 List of chunk dictionaries with text and metadata
 101	 """
 102	 logger.info(f"Chunking text of length {len(text)}")
 103	
 104	 # Clean text
 105	 text = self.clean_text(text)
 106	
 107	 # Detect sections
 108	 sections = self.detect_sections(text)
 109	
 110	 chunks = []
 111	 chunk_id = 0
 112	
 113	 if sections:
 114	 # Chunk each section separately
 115	 for section in sections:
 116	 section_chunks = self.chunk_by_sentences(section['content'])
 117	 for chunk_text in section_chunks:
 118	 chunks.append({
 119	 'chunk_id': chunk_id,
 120	 'text': chunk_text,
 121	 'section': section['title'],
 122	 'char_count': len(chunk_text),
 123	 'content_type': 'text',
 124	 'metadata': metadata or {}
 125	 })
 126	 chunk_id += 1
 127	 else:
 128	 # No sections detected, chunk the entire text
 129	 text_chunks = self.chunk_by_sentences(text)
 130	 for chunk_text in text_chunks:
 131	 chunks.append({
 132	 'chunk_id': chunk_id,
 133	 'text': chunk_text,
 134	 'section': 'unknown',
 135	 'char_count': len(chunk_text),
 136	 'content_type': 'text',
 137	 'metadata': metadata or {}
 138	 })
 139	 chunk_id += 1
 140	
 141	 logger.info(f"Created {len(chunks)} text chunks")
 142	 return chunks
 143	
 144	 def create_table_chunks(
 145	 self, tables: List[Dict], metadata: Dict = None, start_chunk_id: int = 0
 146	 ) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 147	 """
 148	 Create chunks from extracted tables.
 149	
 150	 Each table becomes a single chunk whose text is the Markdown (or CSV)
 151	 representation so it can be embedded and retrieved.
 152	
 153	 Args:
 154	 tables: List of table dicts from Docling (keys: index, html, csv, markdown)
 155	 metadata: Optional metadata to attach
 156	 start_chunk_id: Starting chunk_id to continue numbering
 157	
 158	 Returns:
 159	 List of chunk dictionaries with content_type='table'
 160	 """
 161	 chunks = []
 162	 chunk_id = start_chunk_id
 163	
 164	 for table in tables:
 165	 # Prefer markdown, fall back to csv, then html
 166	 table_text = (
 167	 table.get("markdown")
 168	 or table.get("csv")
 169	 or table.get("html", "")
 170	 )
 171	 if not table_text or not table_text.strip():
 172	 continue
 173	
 174	 chunks.append({
 175	 'chunk_id': chunk_id,
 176	 'text': f"[TABLE {table.get('index', chunk_id)}]\n{table_text}",
 177	 'section': 'table',
 178	 'char_count': len(table_text),
 179	 'content_type': 'table',
 180	 'table_html': table.get('html', ''),
 181	 'metadata': metadata or {},
 182	 })
 183	 chunk_id += 1
 184	
 185	 logger.info(f"Created {len(chunks)} table chunks")
 186	 return chunks
 187	
 188	 def create_image_chunks(
 189	 self, images: List[Dict], metadata: Dict = None, start_chunk_id: int = 0
 190	 ) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 191	 """
 192	 Create chunks from extracted images.
 193	
 194	 Each image becomes a chunk whose text is the caption (used for
 195	 embedding). The base64 PNG data is stored in the chunk metadata so it
 196	 can be forwarded to a multimodal LLM later.
 197	
 198	 Args:
 199	 images: List of image dicts from Docling (keys: base64_png, caption)
 200	 metadata: Optional metadata to attach
 201	 start_chunk_id: Starting chunk_id to continue numbering
 202	
 203	 Returns:
 204	 List of chunk dictionaries with content_type='image'
 205	 """
 206	 chunks = []
 207	 chunk_id = start_chunk_id
 208	
 209	 for idx, img in enumerate(images):
 210	 caption = img.get("caption", "").strip() or f"Figure {idx + 1}"
 211	 # The text used for embedding is the caption / description
 212	 chunks.append({
 213	 'chunk_id': chunk_id,
 214	 'text': f"[FIGURE] {caption}",
 215	 'section': 'figure',
 216	 'char_count': len(caption),
 217	 'content_type': 'image',
 218	 'image_base64': img.get('base64_png', ''),
 219	 'metadata': metadata or {},
 220	 })
 221	 chunk_id += 1
 222	
 223	 logger.info(f"Created {len(chunks)} image chunks")
 224	 return chunks
 225	
 226

 1	"""
 2	Configuration management for the Smart Research Paper Analyzer
 3	"""
 4	from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
 5	from pydantic import Field
 6	from typing import Optional
 7	
 8	
 9	class Settings(BaseSettings):
 10	 """Application settings loaded from environment variables"""
 11

 1	"""
 2	Embedding service using OpenAI for vector generation
 3	"""
 4	from typing import List, Dict
 5	import openai
 6	from loguru import logger
 7	from config import settings
 8	
 9	
 10	class EmbeddingService:
 11	 """Generate embeddings using OpenAI API"""
 12	 
 13	 def __init__(self):
 14	 self.client = openai.OpenAI(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
 15	 self.model = settings.embedding_model
 16	 self.embedding_dimension = 1536 # Default for text-embedding-3-small
 17	 
 18	 def generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
 19	 """
 20	 Generate embedding for a single text
 21	 
 22	 Args:
 23	 text: Input text to embed
 24	 
 25	 Returns:
 26	 List of floats representing the embedding vector
 27	 """
 28	 try:
 29	 response = self.client.embeddings.create(
 30	 model=self.model,
 31	 input=text
 32	 )
 33	 embedding = response.data[0].embedding
 34	 logger.debug(f"Generated embedding of dimension {len(embedding)}")
 35	 return embedding
 36	 except Exception as e:
 37	 logger.error(f"Embedding generation failed: {e}")
 38	 raise
 39	 
 40	 def generate_embeddings_batch(self, texts: List[str], batch_size: int = 100) -> List[List[float]]:
 41	 """
 42	 Generate embeddings for multiple texts in batches
 43	 
 44	 Args:
 45	 texts: List of texts to embed
 46	 batch_size: Number of texts to process in each batch
 47	 
 48	 Returns:
 49	 List of embedding vectors
 50	 """
 51	 embeddings = []
 52	 
 53	 for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size):
 54	 batch = texts[i:i + batch_size]
 55	 logger.info(f"Processing batch {i // batch_size + 1}, size: {len(batch)}")
 56	 
 57	 try:
 58	 response = self.client.embeddings.create(
 59	 model=self.model,
 60	 input=batch
 61	 )
 62	 batch_embeddings = [item.embedding for item in response.data]
 63	 embeddings.extend(batch_embeddings)
 64	 except Exception as e:
 65	 logger.error(f"Batch embedding failed: {e}")
 66	 # Fallback to individual processing
 67	 for text in batch:
 68	 try:
 69	 emb = self.generate_embedding(text)
 70	 embeddings.append(emb)
 71	 except:
 72	 logger.error(f"Failed to embed text: {text[:50]}...")
 73	 embeddings.append([0.0] * self.embedding_dimension)
 74	 
 75	 logger.info(f"Generated {len(embeddings)} embeddings")
 76	 return embeddings
 77	 
 78	 def embed_chunks(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 79	 """
 80	 Add embeddings to chunk dictionaries
 81	 
 82	 Args:
 83	 chunks: List of chunk dictionaries
 84	 
 85	 Returns:
 86	 Chunks with added 'embedding' field
 87	 """
 88	 texts = [chunk['text'] for chunk in chunks]
 89	 embeddings = self.generate_embeddings_batch(texts)
 90	 
 91	 for chunk, embedding in zip(chunks, embeddings):
 92	 chunk['embedding'] = embedding
 93	 
 94	 return chunks

 16	class VectorStore:
 17	 """Qdrant vector database manager"""
 18	 
 19	 def __init__(self):
 20	 # Initialize Qdrant client
 21	 if settings.qdrant_api_key:
 22	 self.client = QdrantClient(
 23	 host=settings.qdrant_host,
 24	 port=settings.qdrant_port,
 25	 api_key=settings.qdrant_api_key
 26	 )
 27	 else:
 28	 self.client = QdrantClient(
 29	 host=settings.qdrant_host,
 30	 port=settings.qdrant_port
 31	 )
 32	 
 33	 self.collection_name = settings.qdrant_collection_name
 34	 self.embedding_dimension = 1536 # OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
 35	 
 36	 # Create collection if it doesn't exist
 37	 self._ensure_collection()
 38	 
 39	 def _ensure_collection(self):
 40	 """Create collection if it doesn't exist, and ensure payload indexes"""
 41	 try:
 42	 collections = self.client.get_collections().collections
 43	 collection_names = [col.name for col in collections]
 44	
 45	 if self.collection_name not in collection_names:
 46	 logger.info(f"Creating collection: {self.collection_name}")
 47	 self.client.create_collection(
 48	 collection_name=self.collection_name,
 49	 vectors_config=VectorParams(
 50	 size=self.embedding_dimension,
 51	 distance=Distance.COSINE
 52	 )
 53	 )
 54	 logger.info("Collection created successfully")
 55	 else:
 56	 logger.info(f"Collection {self.collection_name} already exists")
 57	
 58	 # Create payload indexes so filtered queries work
 59	 self.client.create_payload_index(
 60	 collection_name=self.collection_name,
 61	 field_name="document_id",
 62	 field_schema=models.PayloadSchemaType.KEYWORD,
 63	 )
 64	 self.client.create_payload_index(
 65	 collection_name=self.collection_name,
 66	 field_name="content_type",
 67	 field_schema=models.PayloadSchemaType.KEYWORD,
 68	 )
 69	 logger.info("Payload indexes on 'document_id' and 'content_type' ensured")
 70	
 71	 except Exception as e:
 72	 logger.error(f"Error ensuring collection: {e}")
 73	 raise
 74	 
 75	 def add_documents(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]], document_id: str) -> bool:
 76	 """
 77	 Add document chunks to vector store
 78	 
 79	 Args:
 80	 chunks: List of chunks with embeddings
 81	 document_id: Unique identifier for the document
 82	 
 83	 Returns:
 84	 Success status
 85	 """
 86	 try:
 87	 points = []
 88	 
 89	 for chunk in chunks:
 90	 point_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
 91	 
 92	 content_type = chunk.get('content_type', 'text')
 93	 payload = {
 94	 "document_id": document_id,
 95	 "chunk_id": chunk.get('chunk_id', 0),
 96	 "text": chunk['text'],
 97	 "section": chunk.get('section', 'unknown'),
 98	 "char_count": chunk.get('char_count', 0),
 99	 "content_type": content_type,
 100	 "metadata": chunk.get('metadata', {})
 101	 }
 102	 # Store table HTML for rich rendering
 103	 if content_type == 'table' and chunk.get('table_html'):
 104	 payload["table_html"] = chunk['table_html']
 105	 # Store image base64 for multimodal LLM usage
 106	 if content_type == 'image' and chunk.get('image_base64'):
 107	 payload["image_base64"] = chunk['image_base64']
 108	 
 109	 point = PointStruct(
 110	 id=point_id,
 111	 vector=chunk['embedding'],
 112	 payload=payload
 113	 )
 114	 points.append(point)
 115	 
 116	 # Upload points to Qdrant
 117	 self.client.upsert(
 118	 collection_name=self.collection_name,
 119	 points=points
 120	 )
 121	 
 122	 logger.info(f"Added {len(points)} chunks for document {document_id}")
 123	 return True
 124	 
 125	 except Exception as e:
 126	 logger.error(f"Error adding documents: {e}")
 127	 return False

 129	 def search(
 130	 self,
 131	 query_vector: List[float],
 132	 top_k: int = 5,
 133	 document_id: Optional[str] = None,
 134	 score_threshold: Optional[float] = None
 135	 ) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 136	 """
 137	 Search for similar chunks using query_points (latest Qdrant API)
 138	
 139	 Args:
 140	 query_vector: Query embedding vector
 141	 top_k: Number of results to return
 142	 document_id: Optional filter by document ID
 143	 score_threshold: Minimum similarity score
 144	
 145	 Returns:
 146	 List of search results with text and metadata
 147	 """
 148	 try:
 149	 # Build filter if document_id provided
 150	 search_filter = None
 151	 if document_id:
 152	 search_filter = Filter(
 153	 must=[
 154	 FieldCondition(
 155	 key="document_id",
 156	 match=MatchValue(value=document_id)
 157	 )
 158	 ]
 159	 )
 160	
 161	 # Perform search using query_points (replaces deprecated client.search)
 162	 response = self.client.query_points(
 163	 collection_name=self.collection_name,
 164	 query=query_vector,
 165	 query_filter=search_filter,
 166	 limit=top_k,
 167	 score_threshold=score_threshold,
 168	 with_payload=True,
 169	 )

1. Add config settings for embedding provider

4. For tables: create enhanced text representation before embedding

6. Handle the dimension mismatch issue - different embedding models output different vector sizes (OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small uses 1536 dimensions while Google's text-embedding-004 uses 768), so the user needs to commit to one provider per vector store collection, and I'll need to configure the vector store with the appropriate dimension based on their choice.

Now I'm sketching out the implementation structure - I'll keep the existing OpenAI `EmbeddingService` class as-is, then add support for Google's embedding models through LangChain and direct API calls, making sure each provider option is clearly separated so users can pick whichever fits their needs.

For handling different content types, I'm thinking about how to make images and tables work well with embeddings - for images, I can leverage Gemini's multimodal capabilities to generate descriptions before embedding, and for tables, I'll construct a richer text representation that includes column names and the actual data so the embedding model has better context.

I'll need to add a configuration option to select which embedding provider to use, then update the pipeline to instantiate the right service based on that setting, and I should check that `langchain-google-genai` is available as a dependency.

Now I'm realizing that Google's embedding model outputs 768-dimensional vectors, which means the vector store configuration needs to be flexible to accommodate different embedding dimensions depending on which provider is chosen. I should add an `embedding_dimension` setting to the config to handle this. Looking back at the requirements, the user wants all three embedding options available as choices rather than running simultaneously - they want the flexibility to pick which one to use. So I'll update the `embed_chunks` method to intelligently handle images and tables differently while keeping the provider selection configurable. I'm realizing the current approach is getting too complicated with multimodal descriptions. Let me focus on what's practical: for tables, I'll enhance the text representation with column headers and a summary, and for images, I'll use the caption if available or generate one with Gemini if I have the base64 data. Install with: pip install langchain-google-genai\"\n )\n\n# Google GenAI (already a project dependency)\nfrom google import genai\nfrom google.genai import types as genai_types\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Content-type aware text preparation helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _prepare_text_for_embedding(chunk: Dict) -> str:\n \"\"\"\n Return an embedding-optimised text string based on the chunk's\n content_type (text | table | image).\n \"\"\"\n content_type = chunk.get(\"content_type\", \"text\")\n if content_type == \"table\":\n return _prepare_table_text(chunk)\n elif content_type == \"image\":\n return _prepare_image_text(chunk)\n return chunk.get(\"text\", \"\")\n\n\ndef _prepare_table_text(chunk: Dict) -> str:\n \"\"\"Build a richer textual representation of a table chunk.\"\"\"\n raw = chunk.get(\"text\", \"\")\n # If the chunker already prefixed with [TABLE \u2026], keep it\n if raw.startswith(\"[TABLE\"):\n return raw\n table_html = chunk.get(\"table_html\", \"\")\n return f\"[TABLE] {raw}\" if not table_html else f\"[TABLE]\\n{raw}\"\n\n\ndef _prepare_image_text(chunk: Dict) -> str:\n \"\"\"Build a textual representation of an image chunk (caption based).\"\"\"\n raw = chunk.get(\"text\", \"\")\n if raw.startswith(\"[FIGURE\"):\n return raw\n return f\"[FIGURE] {raw}\"", "old_str_start_line_number_1": 1, "old_str_end_line_number_1": 7}","o|7a|Kv|AX|Kw|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|Kx|9","n|QX","n|GM5","n|BC","n|DPq","o|H2|6D|Kz|L0|L1|H5|L2|d|H8|H9|JY|Kz","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|L3","Now I have all the API details.  151	########################## LANGCHAIN EMBEDDING ######################################
 152	
 153	
 154	class LangChainEmbeddingService:
 155	 """
 156	 Embedding service using LangChain with Google Generative AI
 157	 (model: models/text-embedding-004, 768-d by default).
 158	 """
 159	
 160	 def __init__(self):
 161	 if not LANGCHAIN_AVAILABLE:
 162	 raise RuntimeError(
 163	 "langchain-google-genai is not installed. "
 164	 "Install with: pip install langchain-google-genai"
 165	 )
 166	 self.model_name = getattr(settings, "gemma_embedding_model",
 167	 "models/text-embedding-004")
 168	 self.embeddings = GoogleGenerativeAIEmbeddings(
 169	 model=self.model_name,
 170	 google_api_key=settings.google_api_key,
 171	 )
 172	 self.embedding_dimension = 768
 173	 logger.info(f"LangChain embedding service initialised ({self.model_name})")
 174	
 175	 def generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
 176	 """Generate embedding for a single text."""
 177	 try:
 178	 return self.embeddings.embed_query(text)
 179	 except Exception as e:
 180	 logger.error(f"LangChain embedding failed: {e}")
 181	 raise
 182	
 183	 def generate_embeddings_batch(
 184	 self, texts: List[str], batch_size: int = 100
 185	 ) -> List[List[float]]:
 186	 """Generate embeddings for a batch of texts."""
 187	 all_embeddings: List[List[float]] = []
 188	 for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size):
 189	 batch = texts[i : i + batch_size]
 190	 logger.info(f"LangChain batch {i // batch_size + 1}, size: {len(batch)}")
 191	 try:
 192	 all_embeddings.extend(self.embeddings.embed_documents(batch))
 193	 except Exception as e:
 194	 logger.error(f"LangChain batch embedding failed: {e}")
 195	 for text in batch:
 196	 try:
 197	 all_embeddings.append(self.generate_embedding(text))
 198	 except Exception:
 199	 logger.error(f"Failed to embed text: {text[:50]}...")
 200	 all_embeddings.append([0.0] * self.embedding_dimension)
 201	 logger.info(f"LangChain generated {len(all_embeddings)} embeddings")
 202	 return all_embeddings
 203	
 204	 def embed_chunks(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 205	 """Add embeddings to chunk dicts (content-type aware)."""
 206	 texts = [_prepare_text_for_embedding(c) for c in chunks]
 207	 embeddings = self.generate_embeddings_batch(texts)
 208	 for chunk, emb in zip(chunks, embeddings):
 209	 chunk["embedding"] = emb
 210	 return chunks

","o|IR|X|MC|MH|MW","o|8e|6D|6D|MX","a|MY|LF","toolu_vrtx_01LqKPHsw6Uubb6v5U6bpcN6","{"command": "insert", "path": "backend/services/embedding_service.py", "instruction_reminder": "ALWAYS BREAK DOWN EDITS INTO SMALLER CHUNKS OF AT MOST 150 LINES EACH.", "insert_line_1": 211, "new_str_1": "\n########################## GEMMA / GOOGLE GENAI EMBEDDING ###########################\n\n\nclass GemmaEmbeddingService:\n \"\"\"\n Embedding service using Google GenAI directly.\n Model: text-embedding-004 (768-d).\n Supports native multimodal image embedding via PIL images.\n \"\"\"\n\n def __init__(self):\n self.client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.google_api_key)\n self.model_name = getattr(settings, \"gemma_embedding_model\",\n \"models/text-embedding-004\")\n self.embedding_dimension = 768\n logger.info(f\"Gemma embedding service initialised ({self.model_name})\")\n\n # ---- single text ----\n def generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:\n \"\"\"Generate embedding for a single text string.\"\"\"\n try:\n response = self.client.models.embed_content(\n model=self.model_name,\n contents=text,\n )\n return response.embeddings[0].values\n except Exception as e:\n logger.error(f\"Gemma embedding failed: {e}\")\n raise\n\n # ---- batch text ----\n def generate_embeddings_batch(\n self, texts: List[str], batch_size: int = 100\n ) -> List[List[float]]:\n \"\"\"Generate embeddings for a list of texts.\"\"\"\n all_embeddings: List[List[float]] = []\n for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size):\n batch = texts[i : i + batch_size]\n logger.info(f\"Gemma batch {i // batch_size + 1}, size: {len(batch)}\")\n try:\n response = self.client.models.embed_content(\n model=self.model_name,\n contents=batch,\n )\n all_embeddings.extend([e.values for e in response.embeddings])\n except Exception as e:\n logger.error(f\"Gemma batch embedding failed: {e}\")\n for text in batch:\n try:\n all_embeddings.append(self.generate_embedding(text))\n except Exception:\n logger.error(f\"Failed to embed text: {text[:50]}...\")\n all_embeddings.append([0.0] * self.embedding_dimension)\n logger.info(f\"Gemma generated {len(all_embeddings)} embeddings\")\n return all_embeddings\n\n # ---- image embedding (multimodal) ----\n def generate_image_embedding(self, image_base64: str) -> List[float]:\n \"\"\"\n Generate embedding for an image using Google GenAI multimodal\n embedding. Accepts a base64-encoded PNG string.\n \"\"\"\n try:\n from PIL import Image\n img_bytes = base64.b64decode(image_base64)\n pil_image = Image.open(io.BytesIO(img_bytes))\n response = self.client.models.embed_content(\n model=self.model_name,\n contents=pil_image,\n )\n return response.embeddings[0].values\n except Exception as e:\n logger.warning(f\"Image embedding failed, falling back to caption: {e}\")\n return []\n\n # ---- content-type aware chunk embedding ----\n def embed_chunks(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:\n \"\"\"\n Add embeddings to chunk dicts.\n For image chunks with base64 data, attempts native multimodal\n embedding; falls back to caption text if unavailable.\n \"\"\"\n text_indices: List[int] = []\n text_list: List[str] = []\n\n for idx, chunk in enumerate(chunks):\n ct = chunk.get(\"content_type\", \"text\")\n if ct == \"image\" and chunk.get(\"image_base64\"):\n emb = self.generate_image_embedding(chunk[\"image_base64\"])\n if emb:\n chunk[\"embedding\"] = emb\n continue\n # Fallback / text / table: collect for batch text embedding\n text_indices.append(idx)\n text_list.append(_prepare_text_for_embedding(chunk))\n\n if text_list:\n batch_embs = self.generate_embeddings_batch(text_list)\n for ti, emb in zip(text_indices, batch_embs):\n chunks[ti][\"embedding\"] = emb\n\n return chunks\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Factory \u2013 select embedding provider based on configuration\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nEMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS = {\n \"openai\": 1536,\n \"langchain\": 768,\n \"gemma\": 768,\n}\n\n\ndef get_embedding_service(provider: Optional[str] = None):\n \"\"\"\n Return an embedding service instance for the requested provider.\n\n Args:\n provider: One of 'openai', 'langchain', 'gemma'.\n Defaults to ``settings.embedding_provider``.\n \"\"\"\n provider = (provider or getattr(settings, \"embedding_provider\", \"openai\")).lower()\n\n if provider == \"langchain\":\n logger.info(\"Using LangChain (Google GenAI) embedding provider\")\n return LangChainEmbeddingService()\n elif provider in (\"gemma\", \"google\"):\n logger.info(\"Using Gemma / Google GenAI embedding provider\")\n return GemmaEmbeddingService()\n else:\n logger.info(\"Using OpenAI embedding provider\")\n return EmbeddingService()"}","o|7a|Ma|AX|Mb|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|Mc|9","n|P6","n|HOW","n|VI","n|ESd","n|It","o|H2|6D|Me|Mf|Mg|H5|Mh|d|H8|H9|Mi|Me","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|Mj","o|6l|6n|d|MU|9","a|Md|Mk|Ml|8y|8z","2026-04-08T20:11:49.325Z","o|8b|1K|MU|MV|MZ|Mm|W|Mn|r|X|u","**Step 4:** Update `config.py` with new embedding settings:

 213	########################## GEMMA / GOOGLE GENAI EMBEDDING ###########################
 214	
 215	
 216	class GemmaEmbeddingService:
 217	 """
 218	 Embedding service using Google GenAI directly.
 219	 Model: text-embedding-004 (768-d).
 220	 Supports native multimodal image embedding via PIL images.
 221	 """
 222	
 223	 def __init__(self):
 224	 self.client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.google_api_key)
 225	 self.model_name = getattr(settings, "gemma_embedding_model",
 226	 "models/text-embedding-004")
 227	 self.embedding_dimension = 768
 228	 logger.info(f"Gemma embedding service initialised ({self.model_name})")
 229	
 230	 # ---- single text ----
 231	 def generate_embedding(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
 232	 """Generate embedding for a single text string."""
 233	 try:
 234	 response = self.client.models.embed_content(
 235	 model=self.model_name,
 236	 contents=text,
 237	 )
 238	 return response.embeddings[0].values
 239	 except Exception as e:
 240	 logger.error(f"Gemma embedding failed: {e}")
 241	 raise
 242	
 243	 # ---- batch text ----
 244	 def generate_embeddings_batch(
 245	 self, texts: List[str], batch_size: int = 100
 246	 ) -> List[List[float]]:
 247	 """Generate embeddings for a list of texts."""
 248	 all_embeddings: List[List[float]] = []
 249	 for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size):
 250	 batch = texts[i : i + batch_size]
 251	 logger.info(f"Gemma batch {i // batch_size + 1}, size: {len(batch)}")
 252	 try:
 253	 response = self.client.models.embed_content(
 254	 model=self.model_name,
 255	 contents=batch,
 256	 )
 257	 all_embeddings.extend([e.values for e in response.embeddings])
 258	 except Exception as e:
 259	 logger.error(f"Gemma batch embedding failed: {e}")
 260	 for text in batch:
 261	 try:
 262	 all_embeddings.append(self.generate_embedding(text))
 263	 except Exception:
 264	 logger.error(f"Failed to embed text: {text[:50]}...")
 265	 all_embeddings.append([0.0] * self.embedding_dimension)
 266	 logger.info(f"Gemma generated {len(all_embeddings)} embeddings")
 267	 return all_embeddings
 268	
 269	 # ---- image embedding (multimodal) ----
 270	 def generate_image_embedding(self, image_base64: str) -> List[float]:
 271	 """
 272	 Generate embedding for an image using Google GenAI multimodal
 273	 embedding. Accepts a base64-encoded PNG string.
 274	 """
 275	 try:
 276	 from PIL import Image
 277	 img_bytes = base64.b64decode(image_base64)
 278	 pil_image = Image.open(io.BytesIO(img_bytes))
 279	 response = self.client.models.embed_content(
 280	 model=self.model_name,
 281	 contents=pil_image,
 282	 )
 283	 return response.embeddings[0].values
 284	 except Exception as e:
 285	 logger.warning(f"Image embedding failed, falling back to caption: {e}")
 286	 return []
 287	
 288	 # ---- content-type aware chunk embedding ----
 289	 def embed_chunks(self, chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> List[Dict[str, any]]:
 290	 """
 291	 Add embeddings to chunk dicts.
 292	 For image chunks with base64 data, attempts native multimodal
 293	 embedding; falls back to caption text if unavailable.
 294	 """
 295	 text_indices: List[int] = []
 296	 text_list: List[str] = []
 297	
 298	 for idx, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
 299	 ct = chunk.get("content_type", "text")
 300	 if ct == "image" and chunk.get("image_base64"):
 301	 emb = self.generate_image_embedding(chunk["image_base64"])
 302	 if emb:
 303	 chunk["embedding"] = emb
 304	 continue
 305	 # Fallback / text / table: collect for batch text embedding
 306	 text_indices.append(idx)
 307	 text_list.append(_prepare_text_for_embedding(chunk))
 308	
 309	 if text_list:
 310	 batch_embs = self.generate_embeddings_batch(text_list)
 311	 for ti, emb in zip(text_indices, batch_embs):
 312	 chunks[ti]["embedding"] = emb
 313	
 314	 return chunks
 315	
 316	
 317	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 318	# Factory – select embedding provider based on configuration
 319	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 320	
 321	EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS = {
 322	 "openai": 1536,
 323	 "langchain": 768,
 324	 "gemma": 768,
 325	}
 326	
 327	
 328	def get_embedding_service(provider: Optional[str] = None):
 329	 """
 330	 Return an embedding service instance for the requested provider.
 331	
 332	 Args:
 333	 provider: One of 'openai', 'langchain', 'gemma'.
 334	 Defaults to ``settings.embedding_provider``.
 335	 """
 336	 provider = (provider or getattr(settings, "embedding_provider", "openai")).lower()
 337	
 338	 if provider == "langchain":
 339	 logger.info("Using LangChain (Google GenAI) embedding provider")
 340	 return LangChainEmbeddingService()
 341	 elif provider in ("gemma", "google"):
 342	 logger.info("Using Gemma / Google GenAI embedding provider")
 343	 return GemmaEmbeddingService()
 344	 else:
 345	 logger.info("Using OpenAI embedding provider")
 346	 return EmbeddingService()
