When figures are relevant, mention them by their captions\n5. If information is insufficient, acknowledge it\n6. Be concise but comprehensive\n\nAnswer:\n\"\"\"\n\n answer = self.generate_response(prompt)\n\n return {\n \"answer\": answer,\n \"citations\": citations,\n \"context_used\": len(context_chunks)\n }", "new_str_1": " # -----------------------------------------------------------------\n # Gemini File API helpers\n # -----------------------------------------------------------------\n\n def _upload_files_to_gemini(self, file_paths: List[str]) -> List:\n \"\"\"\n Upload local PDF files to Google's File API so they can be\n referenced inside a generate_content call.\n\n Returns a list of genai File objects (or empty list on failure).\n \"\"\"\n uploaded = []\n for path_str in file_paths:\n path = Path(path_str)\n if not path.exists():\n logger.warning(f\"Source PDF not found, skipping: {path}\")\n continue\n try:\n f = self.client.files.upload(file=path)\n logger.info(f\"Uploaded {path.name} to Gemini ({f.name})\")\n uploaded.append(f)\n except Exception as e:\n logger.warning(f\"Failed to upload {path.name} to Gemini: {e}\")\n return uploaded\n\n # -----------------------------------------------------------------\n # Main Q&A\n # -----------------------------------------------------------------\n\n def answer_question(\n self,\n question: str,\n context_chunks: List[Dict[str, any]],\n source_files: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n source_metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Dict]] = None,\n ) -> Dict[str, any]:\n \"\"\"\n Answer a question based on retrieved context chunks.\n\n Args:\n question: User's question\n context_chunks: Retrieved relevant chunks from vector store\n source_files: Mapping of document_id \u2192 local PDF file path\n source_metadata: Mapping of document_id \u2192 metadata dict\n (keys: filename, title, author, page_count \u2026)\n\n Returns:\n Dictionary with answer, citations, sources, and context_used\n \"\"\"\n source_files = source_files or {}\n source_metadata = source_metadata or {}\n\n # Separate chunks by content type for clearer prompting\n text_parts: List[str] = []\n table_parts: List[str] = []\n image_parts: List[str] = []\n citations: List[Dict] = []\n\n for i, chunk in enumerate(context_chunks):\n ref = f\"[{i+1}]\"\n content_type = chunk.get('content_type', 'text')\n doc_id = chunk.get('document_id', '')\n meta = source_metadata.get(doc_id, {})\n filename = meta.get('filename', '')\n\n if content_type == 'table':\n table_parts.append(f\"{ref} (source: {filename}) {chunk['text']}\")\n elif content_type == 'image':\n image_parts.append(f\"{ref} (source: {filename}) {chunk['text']}\")\n else:\n text_parts.append(f\"{ref} (source: {filename}) {chunk['text']}\")\n\n citations.append({\n \"index\": i + 1,\n \"section\": chunk.get('section', 'unknown'),\n \"score\": chunk.get('score', 0.0),\n \"document_id\": doc_id,\n \"filename\": filename,\n \"text\": chunk.get('text', ''),\n \"content_type\": content_type,\n })\n\n # Assemble context sections\n context_sections: List[str] = []\n if text_parts:\n context_sections.append(\"TEXT EXCERPTS:\\n\" + \"\\n\\n\".join(text_parts))\n if table_parts:\n context_sections.append(\"TABLES:\\n\" + \"\\n\\n\".join(table_parts))\n if image_parts:\n context_sections.append(\"FIGURES/IMAGES:\\n\" + \"\\n\\n\".join(image_parts))\n\n context = \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(context_sections)\n\n # Build source file summary for the prompt\n source_summary_lines: List[str] = []\n for doc_id, meta in source_metadata.items():\n line = (\n f\"- {meta.get('filename', 'unknown')} \"\n f\"(title: {meta.get('title', 'N/A')}, \"\n f\"author: {meta.get('author', 'N/A')}, \"\n f\"pages: {meta.get('page_count', '?')})\"\n )\n source_summary_lines.append(line)\n source_summary = \"\\n\".join(source_summary_lines) if source_summary_lines else \"N/A\"\n\n # Create prompt\n prompt_text = f\"\"\"\nYou are an AI assistant helping researchers analyze academic papers.\nFirst identify the intent of the question, reformulate a new properly articulated question for yourself if needed and answer based on that.\nAnswer the following question based ONLY on the provided context from research papers.\nIf there is no relevant information in the context, clearly state it and add a section for the answer where you use your own knowledge base with a warning that it might not be 100% correct.\nThe context may include regular text excerpts, structured tables, and figure/image captions.\nAlways cite your sources using the reference numbers [1], [2], etc.\n\nWhen the answer involves data from a TABLE, present the relevant data clearly\n(you may re-format as a table if it helps readability).\n\nSource documents:\n{source_summary}\n\nContext from research papers:\n{context}\n\nQuestion: {question}\n\nInstructions:\n1. Provide a clear, accurate answer based on the context\n2. Cite specific sources using [1], [2], etc.\n3. When tables are relevant, reference and summarize the tabular data\n4. When figures are relevant, mention them by their captions\n5. If information is insufficient, acknowledge it\n6. Be concise but comprehensive\n7. When mentioning a source document, include its filename\n\nAnswer:\n\"\"\"\n\n # Upload relevant PDFs to Gemini so the model can read them\n gemini_files = self._upload_files_to_gemini(list(source_files.values()))\n\n # Build contents: uploaded files first, then text prompt\n contents: list = []\n for gf in gemini_files:\n contents.append(gf)\n contents.append(prompt_text)\n\n try:\n config = types.GenerateContentConfig(\n temperature=0.3,\n top_p=0.95,\n top_k=40,\n max_output_tokens=2048,\n response_modalities=[\"TEXT\"],\n )\n\n response = self.client.models.generate_content(\n model=self.model_name,\n contents=contents,\n config=config,\n )\n answer = response.text if response.text else \"No response generated\"\n except Exception as e:\n logger.error(f\"Generation error (with files): {e}\")\n # Fallback: try without uploaded files\n answer = self.generate_response(prompt_text, temperature=0.3)\n\n # Build sources list for the response\n sources = [\n {\"document_id\": doc_id, \"filename\": meta.get(\"filename\", \"unknown\"), \"metadata\": meta}\n for doc_id, meta in source_metadata.items()\n ]\n\n return {\n \"answer\": answer,\n \"citations\": citations,\n \"sources\": sources,\n \"context_used\": len(context_chunks),\n }", "old_str_start_line_number_1": 86, "old_str_end_line_number_1": 174}","o|7a|tE|AX|tF|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|tG|9","n|kU","n|RhS","n|LFO","o|H2|6D|tI|tJ|BB|H5|tK|d|H8|aQ|QN|tI","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|tL","o|6l|6n|d|t8|9","a|tH|tM|tN|8y|8z","2026-04-08T23:15:27.969Z","o|8b|1K|t8|t9|tD|tO|W|tP|r|X|u","2646cc73-678c-4ba9-8fbc-b854489c6a31","Successfully edited the file backend/services/llm_service.py.

","o|Hi|X|uh|um|v0|v1|v2","o|8e|6D|6D|v3","a|v4|kM","toolu_vrtx_01WPZs1zpw4H8A6pCv8dFTv5","{"path": "backend/services/llm_service.py", "view_range": [86, 105]}","o|7a|v6|7c|v7|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|v8|9","toolu_vrtx_01Hp9TvqLpx7hfmfxiwk8PXb","{"path": "backend/services/rag_pipeline.py", "view_range": [244, 280]}","o|7a|vA|7c|vB|1K|1K","o|7T|6K|7U|1K|vC|9","n|THu","n|2v","n|N1O","o|H2|6D|dx|vE|vF|H5|vG|d|H8|aQ|NK|dx","o|6Z|6K|6a|1K|vH","o|6l|6L|d|uy|9","a|v9|vD|vI|vJ|7p|7r","2026-04-08T23:17:07.693Z","o|8b|1K|uy|uz|v5|vK|W|vL|r|X|u","All changes are in place. Here's a summary of what was done across **6 files**:

---

### Backend changes

**1. `config.py`** — Added `upload_dir` setting (defaults to `uploads/`)

**2. `services/rag_pipeline.py`** — Two changes:
- **`process_document`** — Saves the uploaded PDF to `uploads/{document_id}.pdf` on disk
- **`query`** — Collects unique `document_id`s from retrieved chunks, resolves their PDF file paths and metadata, and passes both to `answer_question`

**3. `services/llm_service.py`** — Major update to `answer_question`:
- New `_upload_files_to_gemini()` helper — uploads local PDFs to Google's File API
- `answer_question` now accepts `source_files` (doc_id → file path) and `source_metadata` (doc_id → metadata dict)
- Uploads the relevant PDFs to Gemini so the model can **read the actual documents**, not just text chunks
- Adds a **source document summary** (filename, title, author, pages) to the prompt
- Each citation now includes `filename`
- Returns a `sources` list with full metadata for all referenced documents
- Falls back to text-only generation if file upload fails

**4. `models/schemas.py`** — Added `filename` to `Citation`, new `SourceInfo` model, and `sources` field to `QueryResponse`

### Frontend changes

**5. `frontend/src/types/index.ts`** — Added `filename` to `Citation`, new `SourceInfo` interface, `sources` to `ChatMessage` and `QueryResponse`

---

### How the query flow now works

1. User asks a question
2. Retrieved chunks are grouped by `document_id`
3. For each unique source document, the PDF file is **uploaded to Gemini's File API**
4. Gemini receives both the actual PDFs + structured text chunks + source metadata in the prompt
5. The response includes `citations` with `filename` and `sources` with full metadata

Let me first understand the full requirements:

1. **Tech Stack**: Next.js, DaisyUI for UI elements

 - Markdown rendering for messages

 1	"""
 2	LLM service using Google Gemini API for text generation
 3	Updated to use the latest google.genai library
 4	"""
 5	from typing import List, Dict, Optional
 6	from google import genai
 7	from google.genai import types
 8	from loguru import logger
 9	from config import settings
 10	
 11	
 12	class LLMService:
 13	 """Google Gemini LLM service for generation using google.genai"""
 14	
 15	 def __init__(self):
 16	 # Initialize the Gemini client with API key
 17	 self.client = genai.Client(api_key=settings.google_api_key)
 18	 self.model_name = settings.gemini_model
 19	
 20	 # Default generation configuration
 21	 self.default_config = types.GenerateContentConfig(
 22	 temperature=0.7,
 23	 top_p=0.95,
 24	 top_k=40,
 25	 max_output_tokens=2048,
 26	 response_modalities=["TEXT"],
 27	 )
 28	
 29	 def generate_response(self, prompt: str, temperature: float = 0.7) -> str:
 30	 """
 31	 Generate response from Gemini
 32	
 33	 Args:
 34	 prompt: Input prompt
 35	 temperature: Sampling temperature (0.0 to 2.0)
 36	
 37	 Returns:
 38	 Generated text response
 39	 """
 40	 try:
 41	 # Create generation config with custom temperature
 42	 config = types.GenerateContentConfig(
 43	 temperature=temperature,
 44	 top_p=0.95,
 45	 top_k=40,
 46	 max_output_tokens=2048,
 47	 response_modalities=["TEXT"],
 48	 )
 49	
 50	 # Generate content
 51	 response = self.client.models.generate_content(
 52	 model=self.model_name,
 53	 contents=prompt,
 54	 config=config
 55	 )
 56	
 57	 # Extract text from response
 58	 if response.text:
 59	 return response.text
 60	 else:
 61	 logger.warning("Empty response from Gemini")
 62	 return "No response generated"
 63	
 64	 except Exception as e:
 65	 logger.error(f"Generation error: {e}")
 66	 return f"Error generating response: {str(e)}"
 67	 
 68	 def generate_summary(self, text: str, max_length: int = 500) -> str:
 69	 """Generate a summary of the given text (may include tables/figures)"""
 70	 prompt = f"""
 71	Please provide a concise summary of the following research paper content.
 72	The content may include regular text, structured tables, and figure/image captions.
 73	Focus on the key findings, methodology, and conclusions.
 74	If tables are present, incorporate their key data points into the summary.
 75	If figures are mentioned, reference their significance.
 76	Keep the summary under {max_length} words.
 77	
 78	Content:
 79	{text}
 80	
 81	Summary:
 82	"""
 83	 return self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.5)
 84	 
 85	 def answer_question(self, question: str, context_chunks: List[Dict[str, any]]) -> Dict[str, any]:
 86	 """
 87	 Answer a question based on retrieved context chunks.
 88	
 89	 Handles three content types returned by the vector store:
 90	 - text → regular paper prose
 91	 - table → structured table data (markdown / HTML)
 92	 - image → figure captions (base64 available in payload)
 93	
 94	 Args:
 95	 question: User's question
 96	 context_chunks: Retrieved relevant chunks from vector store
 97	
 98	 Returns:
 99	 Dictionary with answer and citations
 100	 """
 101	 # Separate chunks by content type for clearer prompting
 102	 text_parts = []
 103	 table_parts = []
 104	 image_parts = []
 105	 citations = []
 106	
 107	 for i, chunk in enumerate(context_chunks):
 108	 ref = f"[{i+1}]"
 109	 content_type = chunk.get('content_type', 'text')
 110	
 111	 if content_type == 'table':
 112	 table_parts.append(f"{ref} {chunk['text']}")
 113	 elif content_type == 'image':
 114	 image_parts.append(f"{ref} {chunk['text']}")
 115	 else:
 116	 text_parts.append(f"{ref} {chunk['text']}")
 117	
 118	 citations.append({
 119	 "index": i + 1,
 120	 "section": chunk.get('section', 'unknown'),
 121	 "score": chunk.get('score', 0.0),
 122	 "document_id": chunk.get('document_id', ''),
 123	 "content_type": content_type,
 124	 })
 125	
 126	 # Assemble context sections
 127	 context_sections = []
 128	 if text_parts:
 129	 context_sections.append("TEXT EXCERPTS:\n" + "\n\n".join(text_parts))
 130	 if table_parts:
 131	 context_sections.append("TABLES:\n" + "\n\n".join(table_parts))
 132	 if image_parts:
 133	 context_sections.append("FIGURES/IMAGES:\n" + "\n\n".join(image_parts))
 134	
 135	 context = "\n\n---\n\n".join(context_sections)
 136	
 137	 # Create prompt
 138	 prompt = f"""
 139	You are an AI assistant helping researchers analyze academic papers.
 140	Identify the questions intent and reformulate a more articulated question if needed.
 141	If there is no relevant contex then use your knowledgebase for answering the question , but put a warning that it is not based on the provided source.
 142	Answer the following question based ONLY on the provided context from research papers.
 143	The context may include regular text excerpts, structured tables, and figure/image captions.
 144	If the context doesn't contain enough information to answer the question, say so.
 145	Always cite your sources using the reference numbers [1], [2], etc.
 146	
 147	When the answer involves data from a TABLE, present the relevant data clearly
 148	(you may re-format as a table if it helps readability).
 149	
 150	Context from research papers:
 151	{context}
 152	
 153	Question: {question}
 154	
 155	Instructions:
 156	1. Provide a clear, accurate answer based on the context
 157	2. Cite specific sources using [1], [2], etc.
 158	3. When tables are relevant, reference and summarize the tabular data
 159	4. When figures are relevant, mention them by their captions
 160	5. If information is insufficient, acknowledge it
 161	6. Be concise but comprehensive
 162	
 163	Answer:
 164	"""
 165	
 166	 answer = self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.3)
 167	
 168	 return {
 169	 "answer": answer,
 170	 "citations": citations,
 171	 "context_used": len(context_chunks)
 172	 }
 173	 
 174	 def extract_key_findings(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
 175	 """Extract key findings from research paper text"""
 176	 prompt = f"""
 177	Analyze the following research paper text and extract the key findings.
 178	List them as bullet points, focusing on the most important discoveries and conclusions.
 179	
 180	Text:
 181	{text}
 182	
 183	Key Findings (as bullet points):
 184	"""
 185	 response = self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.4)
 186	 
 187	 # Parse bullet points
 188	 findings = [line.strip() for line in response.split('\n') if line.strip().startswith(('-', '•', '*'))]
 189	 return findings
 190	 
 191	 def identify_methodology(self, text: str) -> str:
 192	 """Identify and summarize the methodology used in the paper"""
 193	 prompt = f"""
 194	Analyze the following research paper text and describe the methodology used.
 195	Focus on the research methods, techniques, and approaches employed.
 196	
 197	Text:
 198	{text}
 199	
 200	Methodology Summary:
 201	"""
 202	 return self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.4)
 203	 
 204	 def extract_limitations(self, text: str) -> List[str]:
 205	 """Extract limitations mentioned in the paper"""
 206	 prompt = f"""
 207	Analyze the following research paper text and identify any limitations mentioned by the authors.
 208	List them as bullet points.
 209	
 210	Text:
 211	{text}
 212	
 213	Limitations (as bullet points):
 214	"""
 215	 response = self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.4)
 216	 
 217	 # Parse bullet points
 218	 limitations = [line.strip() for line in response.split('\n') if line.strip().startswith(('-', '•', '*'))]
 219	 return limitations
 220	
 221

","o|Hi|X|zp|zq|LP|10e|10f","o|8e|6D|6D|10g","n|VGUyZ4s","Here's the result of running `cat -n` on backend/services/rag_pipeline.py:
 1	"""
 2	RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Pipeline
 3	Combines all services for end-to-end document processing and querying
 4	"""
 5	from typing import Dict, List, Optional
 6	import uuid
 7	from loguru import logger
 8	
 9	from services.pdf_parser import PDFParser
 10	from services.chunker import Chunker
 11	from services.embedding_service import get_embedding_service, EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS
 12	from services.vector_store import VectorStore
 13	from services.llm_service import LLMService
 14	from config import settings
 15	
 16	
 17	class RAGPipeline:
 18	 """Main RAG pipeline orchestrating all services"""
 19	 
 20	 def __init__(self):
 21	 self.pdf_parser = PDFParser()
 22	 self.text_chunker = Chunker(
 23	 chunk_size=settings.chunk_size,
 24	 chunk_overlap=settings.chunk_overlap
 25	 )
 26	 self.embedding_service = get_embedding_service()
 27	 self.vector_store = VectorStore()
 28	 self.llm_service = LLMService()
 29	 
 30	 logger.info("RAG Pipeline initialized")
 31	
 32	 def set_embedding_service(self, provider: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 33	 """
 34	 Switch the active embedding provider at runtime.
 35	
 36	 If the new provider uses a different vector dimension the Qdrant
 37	 collection is automatically dropped and recreated so that the
 38	 stored vectors stay consistent with the active embeddings.
 39	
 40	 Args:
 41	 provider: One of 'openai', 'langchain', 'gemma'
 42	
 43	 Returns:
 44	 Dict with success status, provider name, model, and dimension
 45	 """
 46	 try:
 47	 new_service = get_embedding_service(provider)
 48	 old_dimension = self.embedding_service.embedding_dimension
 49	 new_dimension = new_service.embedding_dimension
 50	
 51	 self.embedding_service = new_service
 52	
 53	 # Recreate the Qdrant collection when the dimension changes
 54	 if new_dimension != old_dimension:
 55	 logger.warning(
 56	 f"Embedding dimension changed ({old_dimension} → {new_dimension}). "
 57	 "Recreating Qdrant collection – all existing vectors will be removed."
 58	 )
 59	 self.vector_store.recreate_collection(new_dimension)
 60	
 61	 provider_lower = provider.lower()
 62	 return {
 63	 "success": True,
 64	 "provider": provider_lower,
 65	 "model": getattr(new_service, "model", None)
 66	 or getattr(new_service, "model_name", "unknown"),
 67	 "dimension": new_dimension,
 68	 }
 69	 except Exception as e:
 70	 logger.error(f"Failed to switch embedding provider: {e}")
 71	 return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
 72	
 73	 def get_embedding_info(self) -> Dict[str, any]:
 74	 """Return information about the currently active embedding service."""
 75	 svc = self.embedding_service
 76	 cls_name = type(svc).__name__
 77	 provider_map = {
 78	 "EmbeddingService": "openai",
 79	 "LangChainEmbeddingService": "langchain",
 80	 "GemmaEmbeddingService": "gemma",
 81	 }
 82	 return {
 83	 "provider": provider_map.get(cls_name, cls_name),
 84	 "model": getattr(svc, "model", None)
 85	 or getattr(svc, "model_name", "unknown"),
 86	 "dimension": svc.embedding_dimension,
 87	 "available_providers": list(EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS.keys()),
 88	 }
 89	
 90	 def process_document(self, pdf_file: bytes, filename: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 91	 """
 92	 Process a PDF document through the entire pipeline
 93	 
 94	 Args:
 95	 pdf_file: PDF file as bytes
 96	 filename: Original filename
 97	 
 98	 Returns:
 99	 Processing result with document_id and statistics
 100	 """
 101	 try:
 102	 # Generate unique document ID
 103	 document_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
 104	 logger.info(f"Processing document {filename} with ID {document_id}")
 105	 
 106	 # Step 1: Parse PDF
 107	 logger.info("Step 1: Parsing PDF")
 108	 parsed_data = self.pdf_parser.parse_pdf(pdf_file, filename)
 109	 
 110	 if not parsed_data['text']:
 111	 return {
 112	 "success": False,
 113	 "error": "Failed to extract text from PDF"
 114	 }
 115	 
 116	 # Step 2: Chunk text
 117	 logger.info("Step 2: Chunking text")
 118	 doc_metadata = {
 119	 "filename": filename,
 120	 "title": parsed_data['metadata'].get('title', 'Unknown'),
 121	 "author": parsed_data['metadata'].get('author', 'Unknown'),
 122	 "page_count": parsed_data['page_count']
 123	 }
 124	
 125	 chunks = self.text_chunker.chunk_text(
 126	 parsed_data['text'],
 127	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 128	 )
 129	
 130	 # Step 2b: Create table chunks
 131	 tables = parsed_data.get('tables', [])
 132	 table_chunks = self.text_chunker.create_table_chunks(
 133	 tables,
 134	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 135	 start_chunk_id=len(chunks),
 136	 )
 137	 chunks.extend(table_chunks)
 138	
 139	 # Step 2c: Create image chunks
 140	 images = parsed_data.get('images', [])
 141	 image_chunks = self.text_chunker.create_image_chunks(
 142	 images,
 143	 metadata=doc_metadata,
 144	 start_chunk_id=len(chunks),
 145	 )
 146	 chunks.extend(image_chunks)
 147	
 148	 if not chunks:
 149	 return {
 150	 "success": False,
 151	 "error": "Failed to create chunks"
 152	 }
 153	
 154	 # Step 3: Generate embeddings
 155	 logger.info("Step 3: Generating embeddings")
 156	 chunks_with_embeddings = self.embedding_service.embed_chunks(chunks)
 157	
 158	 # Step 4: Store in vector database
 159	 logger.info("Step 4: Storing in vector database")
 160	 success = self.vector_store.add_documents(chunks_with_embeddings, document_id)
 161	
 162	 if not success:
 163	 return {
 164	 "success": False,
 165	 "error": "Failed to store in vector database"
 166	 }
 167	
 168	 # Return success with statistics
 169	 return {
 170	 "success": True,
 171	 "document_id": document_id,
 172	 "filename": filename,
 173	 "metadata": parsed_data['metadata'],
 174	 "statistics": {
 175	 "page_count": parsed_data['page_count'],
 176	 "char_count": parsed_data['char_count'],
 177	 "chunk_count": len(chunks),
 178	 "table_count": len(table_chunks),
 179	 "image_count": len(image_chunks),
 180	 }
 181	 }
 182	 
 183	 except Exception as e:
 184	 logger.error(f"Document processing error: {e}")
 185	 return {
 186	 "success": False,
 187	 "error": str(e)
 188	 }
 189	 
 190	 def query(
 191	 self, 
 192	 question: str, 
 193	 document_id: Optional[str] = None,
 194	 top_k: int = None
 195	 ) -> Dict[str, any]:
 196	 """
 197	 Query the knowledge base with a question
 198	 
 199	 Args:
 200	 question: User's question
 201	 document_id: Optional specific document to search
 202	 top_k: Number of chunks to retrieve
 203	 
 204	 Returns:
 205	 Answer with citations and context
 206	 """
 207	 try:
 208	 if top_k is None:
 209	 top_k = settings.top_k_results
 210	 
 211	 logger.info(f"Processing query: {question}")
 212	 
 213	 # Step 1: Generate query embedding
 214	 logger.info("Step 1: Generating query embedding")
 215	 query_embedding = self.embedding_service.generate_embedding(question)
 216	 
 217	 # Step 2: Retrieve relevant chunks
 218	 logger.info("Step 2: Retrieving relevant chunks")
 219	 retrieved_chunks = self.vector_store.search(
 220	 query_vector=query_embedding,
 221	 top_k=top_k,
 222	 document_id=document_id,
 223	 score_threshold=settings.similarity_threshold
 224	 )
 225	 
 226	 if not retrieved_chunks:
 227	 return {
 228	 "success": False,
 229	 "answer": "No relevant information found in the knowledge base.",
 230	 "citations": [],
 231	 "context_used": 0
 232	 }
 233	 
 234	 # Step 3: Generate answer using LLM
 235	 logger.info("Step 3: Generating answer")
 236	 result = self.llm_service.answer_question(question, retrieved_chunks)
 237	 
 238	 result["success"] = True
 239	 result["question"] = question
 240	 
 241	 return result
 242	 
 243	 except Exception as e:
 244	 logger.error(f"Query error: {e}")
 245	 return {
 246	 "success": False,
 247	 "error": str(e)
 248	 }
 249	
 250	 def analyze_document(self, document_id: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 251	 """
 252	 Perform comprehensive analysis of a document
 253	
 254	 Args:
 255	 document_id: Document to analyze
 256	
 257	 Returns:
 258	 Analysis results including summary, findings, methodology, limitations
 259	 """
 260	 try:
 261	 logger.info(f"Analyzing document {document_id}")
 262	
 263	 # Retrieve all chunks for the document using scroll (no dummy vector needed)
 264	 all_chunks = self.vector_store.get_by_document_id(document_id)
 265	
 266	 if not all_chunks:
 267	 return {
 268	 "success": False,
 269	 "error": "Document not found"
 270	 }
 271	
 272	 # Separate chunks by content type
 273	 text_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type', 'text') == 'text']
 274	 table_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type') == 'table']
 275	 image_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get('content_type') == 'image']
 276	
 277	 # Combine text from chunks (use first 20 text chunks)
 278	 full_text = " ".join([chunk['text'] for chunk in text_chunks[:20]])
 279	
 280	 # Append table data to context so the LLM can reference it
 281	 if table_chunks:
 282	 table_text = "\n\n".join([c['text'] for c in table_chunks[:10]])
 283	 full_text += f"\n\n--- TABLES FOUND IN THE PAPER ---\n{table_text}"
 284	
 285	 # Append image/figure captions
 286	 if image_chunks:
 287	 fig_text = "\n".join([c['text'] for c in image_chunks[:10]])
 288	 full_text += f"\n\n--- FIGURES/IMAGES IN THE PAPER ---\n{fig_text}"
 289	
 290	 # Generate various analyses
 291	 logger.info("Generating summary")
 292	 summary = self.llm_service.generate_summary(full_text)
 293	
 294	 logger.info("Extracting key findings")
 295	 key_findings = self.llm_service.extract_key_findings(full_text)
 296	
 297	 logger.info("Identifying methodology")
 298	 methodology = self.llm_service.identify_methodology(full_text)
 299	
 300	 logger.info("Extracting limitations")
 301	 limitations = self.llm_service.extract_limitations(full_text)
 302	
 303	 return {
 304	 "success": True,
 305	 "document_id": document_id,
 306	 "summary": summary,
 307	 "key_findings": key_findings,
 308	 "methodology": methodology,
 309	 "limitations": limitations,
 310	 "metadata": all_chunks[0].get('metadata', {}) if all_chunks else {}
 311	 }
 312	
 313	 except Exception as e:
 314	 logger.error(f"Analysis error: {e}")
 315	 return {
 316	 "success": False,
 317	 "error": str(e)
 318	 }
 319	
 320	 def delete_document(self, document_id: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 321	 """Delete a document from the vector store"""
 322	 try:
 323	 success = self.vector_store.delete_document(document_id)
 324	 return {
 325	 "success": success,
 326	 "document_id": document_id
 327	 }
 328	 except Exception as e:
 329	 logger.error(f"Delete error: {e}")
 330	 return {
 331	 "success": False,
 332	 "error": str(e)
 333	 }
 334	
 335	 def get_stats(self) -> Dict[str, any]:
 336	 """Get statistics about the vector store"""
 337	 try:
 338	 info = self.vector_store.get_collection_info()
 339	 return {
 340	 "success": True,
 341	 "stats": info
 342	 }
 343	 except Exception as e:
 344	 logger.error(f"Stats error: {e}")
 345	 return {
 346	 "success": False,
 347	 "error": str(e)
 348	 }

# Install daisyUI for Next.js How to install Tailwind CSS and daisyUI in a Next.js project 

 

### 1. Create a new Next.js project Create a new Next.js project in the current directory

Terminal

 

```
npm create next-app@latest ./
``` ### 2. Install Tailwind CSS and daisyUI

Terminal ```
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss daisyui@latest
```

 

### 3. Add Tailwind CSS and daisyUI Add Tailwind CSS to your PostCSS config file

postcss.config.mjs ... Put Tailwind CSS and daisyUI in your CSS file (and remove old styles)

app/globals.css ```
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "daisyui";
```

 

Now you can use daisyUI class names! ... If you'

DaisyUI 5.x uses Tailwind CSS 4 with the new `@import "tailwindcss"` and `@plugin "daisyui"` syntax. Let me plan the full implementation.

Key DaisyUI 5 setup:
1. Install: `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss daisyui@latest`
2. postcss.config.mjs: use `@tailwindcss/postcss`
3. globals.css: `@import "tailwindcss"; @plugin "daisyui";`


4. Tailwind v4 handles configuration through CSS, so no separate config file is needed.

For the UI, I'll leverage DaisyUI components like drawers for the sidebar, chat bubbles for messages, buttons, file inputs for uploads, textareas for research queries, loading spinners, badges for scores and citations, modals for the PDF viewer, collapsible sections, and menus for displaying sources.

Now I'm mapping out the backend structure.

I notice the Citation schema is missing some fields—it needs text, filename, content_type, and page added back. On the API side, I need to create research endpoints to save and retrieve research topics, plus a scoring endpoint that evaluates documents against the research context. The LLMService will need a scoring method, and the RAGPipeline should store the research context for reference during processing.

For the frontend, I'm setting up a standard Next.js structure with components for the sources sidebar, research panel, chat area, markdown rendering, and PDF viewer, along with utility files for API calls and type definitions.

This is a substantial undertaking, so I'm breaking it into phases—starting with backend changes since the frontend depends on them. First, I'll update the Citation schema with the missing fields, then build out the research endpoints and integrate scoring into the pipeline. I'm focusing on the LLM scoring method for research documents, then setting up the frontend with Tailwind v4 and DaisyUI 5 which requires removing the old config files and updating the build pipeline. Parse bullet points\n limitations = [line.strip() for line in response.split('\\n') if line.strip().startswith(('-', '\u2022', '*'))]\n return limitations\n\n # \u2500\u2500 Research helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n def generate_research_breakdown(self, topic: str, description: str) -> str:\n \"\"\"\n Generate a detailed breakdown of the research topic that can be\n used later to score uploaded documents for relevance.\n \"\"\"\n prompt = f\"\"\"\nYou are a research methodology expert. Given the following research topic and\ndescription, produce a **detailed breakdown** that covers:\n\n1. Core research questions\n2. Key themes and sub-topics\n3. Relevant methodologies and approaches\n4. Expected data types and sources\n5. Important keywords and phrases\n6. Related academic fields and disciplines\n\nResearch topic: {topic}\nDescription: {description}\n\nProvide the breakdown in structured Markdown so it can be used later to\nevaluate how relevant a given paper is to this research.\n\nBreakdown:\n\"\"\"\n return self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.4)\n\n def score_document_relevance(\n self, doc_text: str, filename: str,\n research_topic: str, research_breakdown: str\n ) -> Dict[str, any]:\n \"\"\"\n Score a document's relevance to the research on a 0-100 scale.\n\n Returns dict with keys: score (int), explanation (str).\n \"\"\"\n prompt = f\"\"\"\nYou are an academic research evaluator. Score the following document's\nrelevance to the given research topic on a scale of 0 to 100.\n\nResearch topic: {research_topic}\n\nResearch breakdown:\n{research_breakdown}\n\nDocument filename: {filename}\nDocument content (excerpt):\n{doc_text[:6000]}\n\nReturn your response in EXACTLY this format (no other text):\nSCORE: <number 0-100>\nEXPLANATION: <one paragraph explaining the score>\n\"\"\"\n response = self.generate_response(prompt, temperature=0.2)\n\n # Parse score\n score = 50 # default\n explanation = response\n for line in response.strip().split('\\n'):\n if line.strip().upper().startswith('SCORE:'):\n try:\n score = int(''.join(c for c in line.split(':', 1)[1] if c.isdigit())[:3])\n score = max(0, min(100, score))\n except Exception:\n pass\n elif line.strip().upper().startswith('EXPLANATION:'):\n explanation = line.split(':', 1)[1].strip()\n\n return {\"score\": score, \"explanation\": explanation}", "old_str_start_line_number_1": 207, "old_str_end_line_number_1": 222}","o|7a|14b|AX|14c|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|14d|9","n|Hq","n|WiF","n|8L","n|Slw","o|H2|6D|14f|14g|14h|H5|14i|d|H8|zf|QN|14f","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|14j","Now add the research scoring methods at the end of llm_service.py:

 351	 # ── Research context ──────────────────────────────────────────
 352	
 353	 def set_research(self, topic: str, description: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 354	 """
 355	 Store the research context and generate a detailed breakdown
 356	 using the LLM. The breakdown is cached for document scoring.
 357	 """
 358	 try:
 359	 logger.info(f"Setting research topic: {topic}")
 360	 breakdown = self.llm_service.generate_research_breakdown(topic, description)
 361	 self._research = {
 362	 "topic": topic,
 363	 "description": description,
 364	 "breakdown": breakdown,
 365	 }
 366	 logger.info("Research breakdown generated and stored")
 367	 return {
 368	 "success": True,
 369	 "topic": topic,
 370	 "description": description,
 371	 "breakdown": breakdown,
 372	 }
 373	 except Exception as e:
 374	 logger.error(f"Research error: {e}")
 375	 return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
 376	
 377	 def get_research(self) -> Dict[str, any]:
 378	 """Return the currently stored research context."""
 379	 r = getattr(self, "_research", None)
 380	 if not r:
 381	 return {"success": True, "topic": "", "description": "", "breakdown": ""}
 382	 return {"success": True, **r}
 383	
 384	 def score_document(self, document_id: str) -> Dict[str, any]:
 385	 """Score a document against the current research context."""
 386	 try:
 387	 r = getattr(self, "_research", None)
 388	 if not r or not r.get("topic"):
 389	 return {
 390	 "success": False,
 391	 "error": "No research topic set. Set a research topic first.",
 392	 }
 393	
 394	 # Get document text from vector store
 395	 all_chunks = self.vector_store.get_by_document_id(document_id)
 396	 if not all_chunks:
 397	 return {"success": False, "error": "Document not found"}
 398	
 399	 text_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get("content_type", "text") == "text"]
 400	 full_text = " ".join([c["text"] for c in text_chunks[:15]])
 401	 filename = all_chunks[0].get("metadata", {}).get("filename", "unknown")
 402	
 403	 result = self.llm_service.score_document_relevance(
 404	 full_text, filename, r["topic"], r["breakdown"]
 405	 )
 406	
 407	 return {
 408	 "success": True,
 409	 "document_id": document_id,
 410	 "filename": filename,
 411	 "score": result["score"],
 412	 "explanation": result["explanation"],
 413	 }
 414	 except Exception as e:
 415	 logger.error(f"Scoring error: {e}")
 416	 return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}

\"http://localhost:8000\";\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Upload \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport async function uploadDocument(file: File): Promise<UploadResponse> {\n const fd = new FormData();\n fd.append(\"file\", file);\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/upload`, { method: \"POST\", body: fd });\n if (!res.ok) {\n const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({ error: res.statusText }));\n return { success: false, error: err.detail || err.error || res.statusText };\n }\n return res.json();\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Query \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport async function queryDocuments(\n question: string,\n documentId?: string,\n topK = 5\n): Promise<QueryResponse> {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/query`, {\n method: \"POST\",\n headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n body: JSON.stringify({\n question,\n document_id: documentId || undefined,\n top_k: topK,\n }),\n });\n if (!res.ok) {\n const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({ error: res.statusText }));\n return { success: false, error: err.detail || err.error || res.statusText };\n }\n return res.json();\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Delete \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport async function deleteDocument(\n docId: string\n): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/documents/${docId}`, { method: \"DELETE\" });\n return res.json();\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Research \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport async function setResearch(\n topic: string,\n description: string\n): Promise<ResearchResponse> {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/research`, {\n method: \"POST\",\n headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n body: JSON.stringify({ topic, description }),\n });\n if (!res.ok) {\n const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({ error: res.statusText }));\n return { success: false, error: err.detail || err.error || res.statusText };\n }\n return res.json();\n}\n\nexport async function getResearch(): Promise<ResearchResponse> {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/research`);\n return res.json();\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Score \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport async function scoreDocument(docId: string): Promise<ScoreResponse> {\n const res = await fetch(`${API}/score/${docId}`, { method: \"POST\" });\n if (!res.ok) {\n const err = await res.json().catch(() => ({ error: res.statusText }));\n return { success: false, error: err.detail || err.error || res.statusText };\n }\n return res.json();\n}\n"}","o|7a|1Bx|d2|1By|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1Bz|9","n|G1","n|HLy","n|H86","o|H2|6D|1C1|zw|1C2|H5|1C3|d|H8|zf|jG|1C1","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1C4","a|1C0|1C5|8x|8y|8z","n|1q","n|VGV4QLi","o|Hi|X|1Bc|1Bd|1C7|1C8|ep","o|8e|6D|6D|1C9","a|1CA|1A5","2026-04-11T16:22:29.616Z","o|zo|r|1Bw|1K|W|1C6|u|1CB|1CC|X|1K|C","2a9014bc-85ee-4284-8f34-391807c57fa6","toolu_vrtx_01XBT2ACUKF8TqmbscmRsiqe","{"tasks": IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "frontend/src/components/SourcesSidebar.tsx", "file_content": "\"use client\";\n\nimport React, { useRef } from \"react\";\nimport {\n Plus, FileText, Trash2, Loader2, AlertCircle, CheckCircle2,\n ChevronLeft, ChevronRight,\n} from \"lucide-react\";\nimport type { Source } from \"@/types\";\n\ninterface Props {\n sources: Source[];\n collapsed: boolean;\n onToggle: () => void;\n onAdd: (files: FileList) => void;\n onDelete: (id: string) => void;\n onSelect: (src: Source) => void;\n selectedId?: string;\n}\n\nfunction badge(status: Source[\"status\"]) {\n switch (status) {\n case \"uploading\":\n case \"processing\":\n case \"scoring\":\n return (\n <span className=\"badge badge-sm badge-warning gap-1\">\n <Loader2 size={10} className=\"animate-spin\" />\n {status === \"scoring\" ? \"Scoring\u2026\" : status === \"processing\" ? \"Processing\u2026\" : \"Uploading\u2026\"}\n </span>\n );\n case \"ready\":\n return (\n <span className=\"badge badge-sm badge-success gap-1\">\n <CheckCircle2 size={10} /> Ready\n </span>\n );\n case \"error\":\n return (\n <span className=\"badge badge-sm badge-error gap-1\">\n <AlertCircle size={10} /> Error\n </span>\n );\n }\n}\n\nexport default function SourcesSidebar({\n sources, collapsed, onToggle, onAdd, onDelete, onSelect, selectedId,\n}: Props) {\n const fileRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null);\n\n return (\n <div\n className={`h-full flex flex-col bg-base-200 border-r border-base-300 transition-all duration-200\n ${collapsed ? \"w-12\" : \"w-72\"}`}\n >\n {/* Toggle */}\n <button\n onClick={onToggle}\n className=\"btn btn-ghost btn-sm self-end m-1\"\n aria-label=\"Toggle sources\"\n >\n {collapsed ? <ChevronRight size={16} /> : <ChevronLeft size={16} />}\n </button>\n\n {collapsed ? (\n <div className=\"flex flex-col items-center gap-3 mt-4\">\n <button onClick={() => fileRef.current?.click()} className=\"btn btn-circle btn-sm btn-primary\">\n <Plus size={14} />\n </button>\n {sources.map((s) => (\n <div key={s.documentId} className=\"tooltip tooltip-right\" data-tip={s.filename}>\n <button\n onClick={() => s.status === \"ready\" && onSelect(s)}\n className={`btn btn-circle btn-sm ${s.documentId === selectedId ? \"btn-primary\" : \"btn-ghost\"}`}\n >\n <FileText size={14} />\n </button>\n </div>\n ))}\n </div>\n ) : (\n <>\n {/* Header */}\n <div className=\"px-3 pb-2\">\n <h2 className=\"font-bold text-sm mb-2\">Sources</h2>\n <button\n onClick={() => fileRef.current?.click()}\n className=\"btn btn-primary btn-sm w-full gap-1\"\n >\n <Plus size={14} /> Add PDF\n </button>\n </div>\n\n {/* List */}\n <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-2 space-y-1\">\n {sources.length === 0 && (\n <p className=\"text-xs text-base-content/50 text-center mt-6 px-2\">\n No sources yet. Upload a PDF to get started.\n </p>\n )}\n {sources.map((s) => (\n <div\n key={s.documentId}\n onClick={() => s.status === \"ready\" && onSelect(s)}\n className={`group flex items-start gap-2 p-2 rounded-lg cursor-pointer transition\n ${s.documentId === selectedId ? \"bg-primary/10\" : \"hover:bg-base-300\"}`}\n >\n <FileText size={16} className=\"mt-0.5 shrink-0 opacity-60\" />\n <div className=\"flex-1 min-w-0\">\n <p className=\"text-sm font-medium truncate\">{s.filename}</p>\n <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 mt-0.5\">\n {badge(s.status)}\n {s.score !== undefined && (\n <span className={`badge badge-sm ${s.score >= 70 ? \"badge-success\" : s.score >= 40 ? \"badge-warning\" : \"badge-error\"}`}>\n {s.score}/100\n </span>\n )}\n </div>\n {s.error && <p className=\"text-xs text-error mt-1 truncate\">{s.error}</p>}\n </div>\n {s.status === \"ready\" && (\n <button\n onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); onDelete(s.documentId); }}\n className=\"btn btn-ghost btn-xs opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100\"\n >\n <Trash2 size={12} />\n </button>\n )}\n </div>\n ))}\n </div>\n </>\n )}\n\n <input\n ref={fileRef} type=\"file\" accept=\".pdf\" multiple className=\"hidden\"\n onChange={(e) => e.target.files && onAdd(e.target.files)}\n />\n </div>\n );\n}\n"}","o|7a|1CV|d2|1CW|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1CX|9","n|Q5","n|JiB","n|H8a","o|H2|6D|1CZ|1Ca|12v|H5|1Cb|d|H8|zf|18H|1CZ","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1Cc","Now the components. **SourcesSidebar:**

IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "frontend/src/components/ResearchPanel.tsx", "file_content": "\"use client\";\n\nimport React, { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\nimport { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, Loader2, FlaskConical } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport type { ResearchData } from \"@/types\";\n\ninterface Props {\n collapsed: boolean;\n onToggle: () => void;\n research: ResearchData;\n onSave: (topic: string, description: string) => Promise<void>;\n isSaving: boolean;\n}\n\nexport default function ResearchPanel({\n collapsed, onToggle, research, onSave, isSaving,\n}: Props) {\n const [topic, setTopic] = useState(research.topic);\n const [desc, setDesc] = useState(research.description);\n const dirty = topic !== research.topic || desc !== research.description;\n\n useEffect(() => {\n setTopic(research.topic);\n setDesc(research.description);\n }, [research.topic, research.description]);\n\n const handleSave = () => {\n if (!topic.trim()) return;\n onSave(topic.trim(), desc.trim());\n };\n\n return (\n <div\n className={`h-full flex flex-col bg-base-200 border-l border-base-300 transition-all duration-200\n ${collapsed ? \"w-12\" : \"w-80\"}`}\n >\n {/* Toggle */}\n <button\n onClick={onToggle}\n className=\"btn btn-ghost btn-sm self-start m-1\"\n aria-label=\"Toggle research\"\n >\n {collapsed ? <ChevronLeft size={16} /> : <ChevronRight size={16} />}\n </button>\n\n {collapsed ? (\n <div className=\"flex flex-col items-center mt-4\">\n <div className=\"tooltip tooltip-left\" data-tip=\"Research topic\">\n <button onClick={onToggle} className=\"btn btn-circle btn-sm btn-ghost\">\n <FlaskConical size={16} />\n </button>\n </div>\n </div>\n ) : (\n <div className=\"flex-1 flex flex-col px-3 pb-3 overflow-y-auto\">\n <h2 className=\"font-bold text-sm mb-3 flex items-center gap-1.5\">\n <FlaskConical size={14} /> Research Context\n </h2>\n\n {/* Topic */}\n <label className=\"label text-xs font-semibold\">Topic</label>\n <input\n type=\"text\"\n placeholder=\"e.g. Federated Learning in IoT\"\n value={topic}\n onChange={(e) => setTopic(e.target.value)}\n className=\"input input-bordered input-sm w-full\"\n />\n\n {/* Description */}\n <label className=\"label text-xs font-semibold mt-2\">Description</label>\n <textarea\n placeholder=\"Describe your research goals, methodology, key questions\u2026\"\n value={desc}\n onChange={(e) => setDesc(e.target.value)}\n className=\"textarea textarea-bordered text-sm w-full flex-1 min-h-32 resize-none\"\n />\n\n {/* Save */}\n <button\n onClick={handleSave}\n disabled={!topic.trim() || isSaving || !dirty}\n className=\"btn btn-primary btn-sm mt-3 w-full gap-1\"\n >\n {isSaving ? <Loader2 size={14} className=\"animate-spin\" /> : null}\n {isSaving ? \"Saving\u2026\" : \"Save Research\"}\n </button>\n\n {/* Breakdown preview */}\n {research.breakdown && (\n <div className=\"mt-3\">\n <div className=\"collapse collapse-arrow bg-base-300 rounded-lg\">\n <input type=\"checkbox\" />\n <div className=\"collapse-title text-xs font-semibold py-2 min-h-0\">\n Research Breakdown\n </div>\n <div className=\"collapse-content text-xs leading-relaxed opacity-80 max-h-48 overflow-y-auto\">\n <pre className=\"whitespace-pre-wrap\">{research.breakdown}</pre>\n </div>\n </div>\n </div>\n )}\n </div>\n )}\n </div>\n );\n}\n"}","o|7a|1D6|d2|1D7|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1D8|9","n|K0","n|KBY","n|HhB","o|H2|6D|1DA|1DB|12v|H5|1DC|d|H8|zf|18H|1DA","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1DD","**ResearchPanel** (collapsable right panel):

IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "frontend/src/components/ChatArea.tsx", "file_content": "\"use client\";\n\nimport React, { useRef, useEffect, useState, useMemo, Fragment } from \"react\";\nimport { Send, Loader2, MessageSquare } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport ReactMarkdown from \"react-markdown\";\nimport remarkGfm from \"remark-gfm\";\nimport type { ChatMessage, Citation, Source } from \"@/types\";\n\ninterface Props {\n messages: ChatMessage[];\n isLoading: boolean;\n onSend: (q: string) => void;\n onCitationClick: (c: Citation) => void;\n sources: Source[];\n isEmpty: boolean;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Citation injection \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nfunction injectCitations(\n text: string,\n citations: Citation[] | undefined,\n onClick: (c: Citation) => void\n) {\n if (!citations?.length) return <>{text}</>;\n const parts = text.split(/(\\[\\d+\\])/g);\n return (\n <>\n {parts.map((p, i) => {\n const m = p.match(/^\\[(\\d+)\\]$/);\n if (m) {\n const cit = citations.find((c) => c.index === parseInt(m[1], 10));\n if (cit) {\n return (\n <button\n key={i}\n onClick={() => onClick(cit)}\n className=\"badge badge-sm badge-primary mx-0.5 cursor-pointer hover:badge-secondary\"\n title={`${cit.filename || cit.section}${cit.page ? ` p.${cit.page}` : \"\"} (${(cit.score * 100).toFixed(0)}%)`}\n >\n {p}\n </button>\n );\n }\n }\n return <Fragment key={i}>{p}</Fragment>;\n })}\n </>\n );\n}\n\nfunction walk(\n children: React.ReactNode,\n citations: Citation[] | undefined,\n onClick: (c: Citation) => void\n): React.ReactNode {\n return React.Children.map(children, (child) =>\n typeof child === \"string\" ? injectCitations(child, citations, onClick) : child\n );\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Markdown renderer \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nfunction Md({\n content, citations, onClick,\n}: {\n content: string; citations?: Citation[]; onClick: (c: Citation) => void;\n}) {\n const comp = useMemo(() => ({\n p: ({ children, ...ps }: any) => <p className=\"mb-2 last:mb-0 leading-relaxed\" {...ps}>{walk(children, citations, onClick)}</p>,\n li: ({ children, ...ps }: any) => <li className=\"ml-1\" {...ps}>{walk(children, citations, onClick)}</li>,\n strong: ({ children, ...ps }: any) => <strong className=\"font-bold\" {...ps}>{walk(children, citations, onClick)}</strong>,\n em: ({ children, ...ps }: any) => <em {...ps}>{walk(children, citations, onClick)}</em>,\n h1: ({ children }: any) => <h3 className=\"text-base font-bold mt-3 mb-1\">{children}</h3>,\n h2: ({ children }: any) => <h4 className=\"text-sm font-bold mt-2 mb-1\">{children}</h4>,\n h3: ({ children }: any) => <h5 className=\"text-sm font-semibold mt-2 mb-1\">{children}</h5>,\n ul: ({ children }: any) => <ul className=\"list-disc pl-5 mb-2 space-y-0.5\">{children}</ul>,\n ol: ({ children }: any) => <ol className=\"list-decimal pl-5 mb-2 space-y-0.5\">{children}</ol>,\n table: ({ children }: any) => (\n <div className=\"overflow-x-auto mb-2\"><table className=\"table table-xs\">{children}</table></div>\n ),\n blockquote: ({ children }: any) => (\n <blockquote className=\"border-l-4 border-primary/40 pl-3 italic opacity-80 mb-2\">{children}</blockquote>\n ),\n code: ({ children, className }: any) => {\n const block = className?.includes(\"language-\");\n return block\n ? <pre className=\"mockup-code text-xs mb-2 p-3 overflow-x-auto\"><code>{children}</code></pre>\n : <code className=\"badge badge-ghost text-xs\">{children}</code>;\n },\n hr: () => <div className=\"divider my-1\" />,\n }), [citations, onClick]);\n\n return <ReactMarkdown remarkPlugins={[remarkGfm]} components={comp}>{content}</ReactMarkdown>;\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 ChatArea component \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n\nexport default function ChatArea({ messages, isLoading, onSend, onCitationClick, sources, isEmpty }: Props) {\n const [input, setInput] = useState(\"\");\n const bottomRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n const hasReady = sources.some((s) => s.status === \"ready\");\n\n useEffect(() => {\n bottomRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ behavior: \"smooth\" });\n }, [messages, isLoading]);\n\n const submit = (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n e.preventDefault();\n const q = input.trim();\n if (!q || isLoading) return;\n onSend(q);\n setInput(\"\");\n };\n\n /* Empty state \u2014 centered input */\n if (isEmpty) {\n return (\n <div className=\"flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-6 px-4\">\n <MessageSquare size={56} className=\"opacity-20\" />\n <h2 className=\"text-2xl font-bold opacity-70\">PaperSense</h2>\n <p className=\"text-sm opacity-50 text-center max-w-md\">\n Upload research papers on the left, optionally set your research context on the right, then ask questions below.\n </p>\n <form onSubmit={submit} className=\"join w-full max-w-xl\">\n <input\n type=\"text\" value={input} onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)}\n placeholder={hasReady ? \"Ask about your papers\u2026\" : \"Upload a PDF first\u2026\"}\n disabled={!hasReady} className=\"input input-bordered join-item flex-1\"\n />\n <button type=\"submit\" disabled={!input.trim() || isLoading || !hasReady} className=\"btn btn-primary join-item\">\n <Send size={18} />\n </button>\n </form>\n </div>\n );\n }\n\n /* Chat view */\n return (\n <div className=\"flex-1 flex flex-col h-full\">\n <div className=\"flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-4 space-y-1\">\n {messages.map((msg) => {\n const isUser = msg.role === \"user\";\n return (\n <div key={msg.id} className={`chat ${isUser ? \"chat-end\" : \"chat-start\"}`}>\n <div className={`chat-bubble ${isUser ? \"chat-bubble-primary\" : \"chat-bubble-neutral\"} max-w-[75%]`}>\n {isUser ? (\n <Md content={msg.content} onClick={onCitationClick} />\n ) : (\n <Md content={msg.content} citations={msg.citations} onClick={onCitationClick} />\n )}\n </div>\n </div>\n );\n })}\n {isLoading && (\n <div className=\"chat chat-start\">\n <div className=\"chat-bubble chat-bubble-neutral flex items-center gap-2\">\n <Loader2 size={16} className=\"animate-spin\" /> Thinking\u2026\n </div>\n </div>\n )}\n <div ref={bottomRef} />\n </div>\n\n <form onSubmit={submit} className=\"border-t border-base-300 p-3 flex gap-2\">\n <input\n type=\"text\" value={input} onChange={(e) => setInput(e.target.value)}\n placeholder={hasReady ? \"Ask about your papers\u2026\" : \"Upload a PDF first\u2026\"}\n disabled={!hasReady || isLoading}\n className=\"input input-bordered flex-1 input-sm\"\n />\n <button type=\"submit\" disabled={!input.trim() || isLoading || !hasReady} className=\"btn btn-primary btn-sm\">\n <Send size={16} />\n </button>\n </form>\n </div>\n );\n}\n"}","o|7a|1Dy|d2|1Dz|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1E0|9","n|dM","n|MDa","n|HdH","o|H2|6D|1E2|zw|1E3|H5|1E4|d|H8|zf|12E|1E2","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1E5","Good — I've confirmed the DaisyUI 5 chat bubble API: `chat chat-start`/`chat-end`, `chat-bubble`, `chat-bubble-primary`/`chat-bubble-neutral`, `chat-header`, `chat-footer`. Now I'll create the ChatArea component:

IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "frontend/src/components/PdfViewer.tsx", "file_content": "\"use client\";\n\nimport React, { useState } from \"react\";\nimport { X, FileText, BookOpen, Copy, CheckCircle2 } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport type { Citation, Source } from \"@/types\";\n\ninterface Props {\n isOpen: boolean;\n citation: Citation | null;\n source: Source | null;\n onClose: () => void;\n}\n\nexport default function PdfViewer({ isOpen, citation, source, onClose }: Props) {\n const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);\n if (!isOpen || !source) return null;\n\n const page = citation?.page ?? 1;\n const pdfUrl = `${source.blobUrl}#page=${page}&toolbar=1`;\n\n const handleCopy = () => {\n if (citation?.text) {\n navigator.clipboard.writeText(citation.text);\n setCopied(true);\n setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000);\n }\n };\n\n return (\n <div className=\"fixed inset-0 z-50 flex\">\n {/* Backdrop */}\n <div className=\"absolute inset-0 bg-black/40\" onClick={onClose} />\n\n {/* Panel */}\n <div className=\"relative ml-auto w-full max-w-4xl bg-base-100 shadow-2xl flex flex-col animate-slide-in-right\">\n {/* Header */}\n <div className=\"navbar bg-base-200 min-h-0 px-4 py-2\">\n <div className=\"flex-1 gap-2 min-w-0\">\n <FileText size={16} className=\"shrink-0 opacity-60\" />\n <span className=\"text-sm font-medium truncate\">{source.filename}</span>\n {citation && (\n <span className=\"badge badge-sm badge-outline\">Page {page}</span>\n )}\n </div>\n <button onClick={onClose} className=\"btn btn-ghost btn-sm btn-circle\">\n <X size={16} />\n </button>\n </div>\n\n {/* Citation context */}\n {citation && (\n <div className=\"px-4 py-2 bg-primary/5 border-b border-base-300 space-y-1.5\">\n <div className=\"flex items-center gap-2 text-xs font-semibold text-primary\">\n <BookOpen size={14} />\n Citation [{citation.index}] \u2014 {citation.section}\n <span className=\"ml-auto opacity-60\">\n {(citation.score * 100).toFixed(0)}% match\n </span>\n </div>\n\n {citation.text && (\n <div className=\"relative\">\n <div className=\"bg-base-100 rounded-lg p-2.5 border border-primary/20 text-sm max-h-28 overflow-y-auto leading-relaxed\">\n “{citation.text}”\n </div>\n <button\n onClick={handleCopy}\n className=\"absolute top-1.5 right-1.5 btn btn-ghost btn-xs btn-circle\"\n title=\"Copy\"\n >\n {copied ? <CheckCircle2 size={12} className=\"text-success\" /> : <Copy size={12} />}\n </button>\n <p className=\"text-xs opacity-50 mt-1\">\n Use <kbd className=\"kbd kbd-xs\">Ctrl+F</kbd> in the PDF to find this text.\n </p>\n </div>\n )}\n </div>\n )}\n\n {/* PDF iframe */}\n <div className=\"flex-1 min-h-0\">\n <iframe src={pdfUrl} className=\"w-full h-full border-0\" title={source.filename} />\n </div>\n </div>\n </div>\n );\n}\n"}","o|7a|1EW|d2|1EX|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1EY|9","n|ID","n|O7I","n|Mt8","o|H2|6D|1Ea|1Eb|FV|H5|1Ec|d|H8|zf|18H|1Ea","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1Ed","**PdfViewer** component:
","o|6l|6n|d|1Ef|9","a|1EZ|1Ee|1Eg|8y|8z","n|VGV5YJ3","o|Hi|X|1EF|1EG|18L|1Ei|18N","o|8e|6D|6D|1Ej","a|1Ek|1A5","2026-04-11T16:26:58.525Z","o|zo|r|1EV|1K|W|1Eh|u|1El|1Em|X|1Ef|C","917fd920-79e3-4d43-9f91-8b2f4b9890c9","toolu_vrtx_01Vy3Py2hdmYVL3fYobP5zoK","{"tasks": [{"task_id": "T8w3UreSB3kE1VDzMN4BQY", "state": "COMPLETE"}, {"task_id": "k9KWNhsRgLxFhkJyBYs2nq", "state": "IN_PROGRESS"}]}","o|7a|1Ep|bj|1Eq|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1Er|9","n|O9t","n|Is","n|NFM","o|H2|6D|gd|1Et|1Eu|H5|1Ev|d|H8|zf|g4|gd","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1Ew","a|1Es|1Ex|8x|8y|8z","n|2R","n|VGV5hM1","o|Hi|X|1EV|1EW|1Ez|1F0|gV","o|8e|6D|6D|1F1","a|1F2|1A5","2026-04-11T16:27:33.380Z","o|zo|r|1Eo|1K|W|1Ey|u|1F3|1F4|X|1K|C","4198c12d-e85a-49d1-bed2-df5cd2ae07e3","toolu_vrtx_01QyVmAtgG2Z3sJpdBxrFmjs","{"instructions_reminder": "LIMIT THE FILE CONTENT TO AT MOST 150 LINES. IF MORE CONTENT NEEDS TO BE ADDED USE THE str-replace-editor TOOL TO EDIT THE FILE AFTER IT HAS BEEN CREATED.", "path": "frontend/src/app/page.tsx", "file_content": "\"use client\";\n\nimport React, { useState, useCallback } from \"react\";\nimport SourcesSidebar from \"@/components/SourcesSidebar\";\nimport ResearchPanel from \"@/components/ResearchPanel\";\nimport ChatArea from \"@/components/ChatArea\";\nimport PdfViewer from \"@/components/PdfViewer\";\nimport {\n uploadDocument, queryDocuments, deleteDocument,\n setResearch, scoreDocument,\n} from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport type { Source, ChatMessage, Citation, ResearchData } from \"@/types\";\n\nexport default function Home() {\n /* Sources */\n const [sources, setSources] = useState<Source[]>([]);\n const [selectedSourceId, setSelectedSourceId] = useState<string>();\n\n /* Chat */\n const [messages, setMessages] = useState<ChatMessage[]>([]);\n const [isQuerying, setIsQuerying] = useState(false);\n\n /* Research */\n const [research, setResearchState] = useState<ResearchData>({ topic: \"\", description: \"\" });\n const [isSavingResearch, setIsSavingResearch] = useState(false);\n\n /* Sidebar collapse */\n const [srcCollapsed, setSrcCollapsed] = useState(false);\n const [resCollapsed, setResCollapsed] = useState(false);\n\n /* PDF viewer */\n const [viewerOpen, setViewerOpen] = useState(false);\n const [viewerCitation, setViewerCitation] = useState<Citation | null>(null);\n const [viewerSource, setViewerSource] = useState<Source | null>(null);\n\n /* \u2500\u2500 Sources \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n const handleAddSource = useCallback(async (files: FileList) => {\n for (const file of Array.from(files)) {\n const tempId = crypto.randomUUID();\n const blobUrl = URL.createObjectURL(file);\n const src: Source = { documentId: tempId, filename: file.name, status: \"uploading\", file, blobUrl };\n setSources((p) => [...p, src]);\n\n try {\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) => (s.documentId === tempId ? { ...s, status: \"processing\" } : s)));\n const res = await uploadDocument(file);\n if (res.success && res.document_id) {\n const docId = res.document_id;\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) => (s.documentId === tempId ? { ...s, documentId: docId, status: \"ready\" } : s)));\n\n // Auto-score if research topic exists\n if (research.topic) {\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) => (s.documentId === docId ? { ...s, status: \"scoring\" } : s)));\n const sr = await scoreDocument(docId);\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) =>\n s.documentId === docId\n ? { ...s, status: \"ready\", score: sr.score, scoreExplanation: sr.explanation }\n : s\n ));\n }\n } else {\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) => (s.documentId === tempId ? { ...s, status: \"error\", error: res.error } : s)));\n }\n } catch (err) {\n setSources((p) => p.map((s) => (s.documentId === tempId ? { ...s, status: \"error\", error: String(err) } : s)));\n }\n }\n }, [research.topic]);\n\n const handleDelete = useCallback(async (id: string) => {\n try { await deleteDocument(id); } catch {}\n setSources((p) => { p.find((s) => s.documentId === id && URL.revokeObjectURL(s.blobUrl)); return p.filter((s) => s.documentId !== id); });\n if (selectedSourceId === id) setSelectedSourceId(undefined);\n }, [selectedSourceId]);\n\n /* \u2500\u2500 Research \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n const handleSaveResearch = useCallback(async (topic: string, description: string) => {\n setIsSavingResearch(true);\n try {\n const res = await setResearch(topic, description);\n if (res.success) {\n setResearchState({ topic: res.topic || topic, description: res.description || description, breakdown: res.breakdown });\n\n // Re-score all ready sources\n const readySources = sources.filter((s) => s.status === \"ready\");\n for (const s of readySources) {\n setSources((p) => p.map((x) => (x.documentId === s.documentId ? { ...x, status: \"scoring\" } : x)));\n const sr = await scoreDocument(s.documentId);\n setSources((p) => p.map((x) =>\n x.documentId === s.documentId\n ? { ...x, status: \"ready\", score: sr.score, scoreExplanation: sr.explanation }\n : x\n ));\n }\n }\n } finally {\n setIsSavingResearch(false);\n }\n }, [sources]);\n\n /* \u2500\u2500 Chat \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n const handleSend = useCallback(async (question: string) => {\n const userMsg: ChatMessage = { id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: \"user\", content: question, timestamp: new Date() };\n setMessages((p) => [...p, userMsg]);\n setIsQuerying(true);\n try {\n const res = await queryDocuments(question, selectedSourceId);\n setMessages((p) => [...p, {\n id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: \"assistant\",\n content: res.answer || res.error || \"No response\",\n citations: res.citations, sources: res.sources, timestamp: new Date(),\n }]);\n } catch (err) {\n setMessages((p) => [...p, { id: crypto.randomUUID(), role: \"assistant\", content: `Error: ${err}`, timestamp: new Date() }]);\n } finally { setIsQuerying(false); }\n }, [selectedSourceId]);\n\n /* \u2500\u2500 Citations \u2192 PDF \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n const handleCitation = useCallback((cit: Citation) => {\n const src = sources.find((s) => s.documentId === cit.document_id);\n if (src) { setViewerCitation(cit); setViewerSource(src); setViewerOpen(true); }\n }, [sources]);\n\n /* \u2500\u2500 Render \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\n return (\n <div className=\"flex h-screen bg-base-100\">\n <SourcesSidebar\n sources={sources} collapsed={srcCollapsed} onToggle={() => setSrcCollapsed(!srcCollapsed)}\n onAdd={handleAddSource} onDelete={handleDelete}\n onSelect={(s) => setSelectedSourceId(s.documentId)} selectedId={selectedSourceId}\n />\n <ChatArea\n messages={messages} isLoading={isQuerying} onSend={handleSend}\n onCitationClick={handleCitation} sources={sources} isEmpty={messages.length === 0}\n />\n <ResearchPanel\n collapsed={resCollapsed} onToggle={() => setResCollapsed(!resCollapsed)}\n research={research} onSave={handleSaveResearch} isSaving={isSavingResearch}\n />\n <PdfViewer\n isOpen={viewerOpen} citation={viewerCitation} source={viewerSource}\n onClose={() => setViewerOpen(false)}\n />\n </div>\n );\n}\n"}","o|7a|1F7|d2|1F8|1K|1K","o|7T|6D|7U|1K|1F9|9","n|Xi","n|LzV","n|NE1","o|H2|6D|1FB|zw|1FC|H5|1FD|d|H8|zf|18H|1FB","o|6Z|6D|6a|1K|1FE","**Main page.tsx** Updated `.gitignore`

I rewrote the root `.gitignore` to exclude the main unnecessary files for this repo:

- Python cache/build artifacts
- virtual environments
- Next.js build artifacts and `node_modules`
- local `.env` files
- logs
- uploaded PDFs / local generated data
- IDE / OS junk
- temp/cache files

## Included project-specific ignores

I also covered likely local-only data for this project, including:

- `uploads/`
- `backend/uploads/`
- `logs/`
- `backend/logs/`
- `qdrant_storage/`
- `.qdrant/`

## Important note

If any of these files were **already committed before**, `.gitignore` won’t remove them automatically from Git tracking.

If you want, I can next give you the exact `git rm --cached ...` commands to stop tracking already-committed generated files.
